E: However, Maestro Max... He maintains a sense of innocent purity throughout his self-expression, which puts us at *ease*.

C: 目前来说，我的主要业务是向仙舟引进苏乐达。
E: Currently, my main business is to import SoulGlad to the Xianzhou.

C: 啊…这挥金如土的感觉，可真是该死的甜美啊！
E: Ah... it's a blasted good feeling to spend money as if it meant nothing!

C: 要是有喜欢的，我可以帮您看看折扣，价钱绝对公道。
E: If you see something you like, I can help you check if there's a discount, it'll be a steal!

C: 这…没关系，照样给！谁让咱们都是来匹诺康尼玩的逐梦客呢，哈哈~
E: That... It's fine. I'll give them to you anyway! After all, we are all Dreamchasers here in Penacony, haha~

C: 唉，为什么人只有在梦中才能放松自己呢……
E: Ah, why is it that people can only ever relax once they are in the dream world...

C: 呜呼，真是人不疯玩枉少年啊！
E: Woohoo! No Party, No Life! My friend!

C: 你为啥觉得自己搞不懂二次元？
E: Why do you think you don't understand what anime is?

C: 如果有一天——我想这一天也许不会太久——常盘国的二次元艺术走向了没落，那我身为教育科学省的前大臣，必然是最大的罪人！
E: If the day comes — A day that I don't think is far away — when Tokiwa anime falls into decline, then my time spent as the Minister of Education and Technology was my biggest sin to my people!

C: 可到最后，有人在乎她那些深奥的「艺术理论」吗？没有。人们只在乎大球馆极不合理的卫生间位置，以及缺乏安全性的球场设计。
E: But, in the end, did anyone care about her "artistic theories"? No. People only cared about the extremely illogical locations of the toilets within the large stadium and the unsafe design of the court.

C: …哼哼哼♪…哼、哼哼哼……
E: Hmhmhm♪... Hm... Hmhmhm...

C: 为什么不去梦里调酒？
E: Why aren't you bartending in the Dreamscape?

C: 这…这当然是…当然是梦里哪有这大堂方便交朋友啊！
E: I—It's because... Well, making friends here, in this lobby, is much more convenient than trying to do it in the dream!

C: 此外，请勿轻信「量子穿梭机」、「忆梦安眠饮料」等非官方入梦道具。
E: Please do not trust any unauthorized dreaming devices such as the "Quantum Shuttle" or the "Sweetdream Drink."

C: 昨晚的机动球比赛可真刺激！依我看，苏乐达真该把对面的<unbreak>23</unbreak>号给买过去！
E: The Roboball game last night was thrilling! You know what? SoulGlad seriously should have snagged No. <unbreak>23</unbreak> from the other team!

C: 大家各司其职，我也没兴趣在背后对谁说长道短。
E: We all focus on our respective jobs. Gossiping about others behind their backs is not something I engage in.

C: 似乎是在梦境里…
E: I think we met somewhere in the Dreamscape...

C: 这些粘稠的液体顺着身体表面滴落，最后汇聚成块状的凝胶…从外形上看，是崭新出厂的散热凝胶。
E: Sticky fluids trickle down Jno's body and form gel-like clumps... From the looks of it, it is the latest cooling gel.

C: …请允许我打断您一下，{F#女士}{M#先生}，但您的梦境中似乎出现了几个被列入星际通缉名单的危险人物。
E: ...I apologize for the interruption, {F#miss}{M#sir}, but it appears that some dangerous individuals from the most wanted list have emerged in your dream.

C: 窥梦电话已拨通——您将会听到：黑塔人偶的梦境。
E: Dreampeek Call connected. You are about to hear Herta Puppet's dream.

C: 「窥梦电话」是什么？
E: What is a "Dreampeek Call"?

C: …你以闪电般的速度挂断了电话。
E: ...You hang up faster than lightning.

C: 「窥梦电话」是什么？
E: What is a "Dreampeek Call"?

C: …别别别别。有话好好说，咱们相遇就是缘，买到我的时候你也花了不少钱不是，就这么浪费多可惜。别紧张，别冲动哈。
E: ...Hold on. We can talk this over. Fate has brought us together, and you have spent a fortune on me. It will be a massive waste to destroy me, right? Please calm down and don't be rash.

C: 我收到了邀请。
E: I received an invitation.

C: 你们一人一鸟就这么互相盯着对方，时间在沉默中流逝。
E: Time passes in silence as you and the bird stare at each other.

C: 嗯嗯，行，好，对对对。
E: Yes... Right... Sure... Exactly.

C: 快、快把它拿给我看看！
E: L—Let me take a closer look!

C: 我*皮皮西粗口*！谁让你把这房间布置成这*皮皮西粗口*样的？
E: What the *Pepeshi profanity*! Who asked you to set up this room in this *Pepeshi profanity* way!?

C: 据说有伪装成熊崽的智械、自称为皇帝的游民，甚至某次似乎还有押进来个奇怪的广告牌……
E: I hear there is an Intellitron that impersonates a teddy bear, a homeless man that calls themselves an "Emperor"... I hear they even brought in a weird billboard...

C: 不过我告诉您的那些八卦——您记得为我保密呀！
E: Although, these rumors I've told you... You have to keep them a secret!

C: 真是顶高帽啊。不过既然大家这么看重我，我也得好好回应才行。来吧，尽情朝我发射甜甜圈——我是说，尽情向我倾诉吧。
E: What a pretentious farce. But since everyone thinks so highly of me, I have to reciprocate in kind. Come, fling donuts at me to your heart's content— Err, I mean, confess to me to your heart's content.

C: 就这样吧，拜拜。
E: That's it. Bye-bye.

C: 唉，这年头，喜欢动画片的人越来越少了……
E: *sigh* There are fewer and fewer people who like cartoons these days...

C: 你是哪种打工人？
E: So what kind of employee are you?

C: …或许，我该向家族寻求一些帮助……
E: ...Perhaps, I should seek help from The Family...

C: 或许在未来的某天，全宇宙都会厌倦梦境，连带着厌倦一切幻想…到了那时，我们这些人又该能做些什么呢？
E: There might come a day when the entire universe gets sick of the Dreamscape as well as the illusions that come with it... When the time comes, what can people like us do?

C: 仔细想想，之前的我真是太死板了。这样惬意的日子，就该笑着过才对！
E: Come to think of it, I've been too rigid all this while. I should be enjoying such a wonderful life with a smile!

C: 哈哈，我就说吧！
E: Told you!

C: 咳咳，总之就是很厉害！你只需要接受这个设定就好。
E: Ahem. The armor is awesome. That's all I'm saying! You just need to accept that setting.

C: 我平日乐于助人，偶尔做点无害的幻想。神主认为我是好的，便向我投来注视。这是良善的回报。
E: I enjoyed helping others and occasionally daydreamed harmlessly. The Great One must have thought I was good and decided to cast THEIR gaze upon me. It was the reward for my kindness.

C: 必须抑制这危险的情感。要忍耐…对，忍耐……
E: I have to suppress this dangerous emotion. I need to be patient... pa... tient...

C: 瞧瞧他们，挤破了头都想往这梦里钻……
E: Look at them, they're all desperate to get into this dream...

C: 可以和你聊聊吗？
E: Can we have a chat?

C: …我有时会想，为什么我的父母偏偏是家族成员？为什么我从小就得学习「谐乐颂」，背诵那些其实根本就没人在乎的词句，只为了讨所谓的大人物开心？
E: ...I sometimes think, why did my parents have to be members of The Family? Why did I have to study the "Odes of Harmony" from such a young age? Memorizing and reciting all those words that no one cares about just to please these so-called "important figures"?

C: 嘿，这位客人，欢迎光临钟表餐厅克劳克影视乐园分厅。请问您是想来点——
E: Hey, dear guest! Welcome to the Clock Diner in Clock Studios Theme Park! May I ask what I can get you—

C: 哈哈，雕虫小技而已，您谬赞了。
E: Haha, it's just a little trick I do, it's not that big a deal.

C: 哈哈，客人您真是上道。要来份「酸梦」软糖吗？便宜实惠、酸爽可口，保证您喜欢！
E: Haha, you know your stuff, dear customer. How about some "Sour Dreams" Soft Candy? They're great value for money, sour and refreshing, I guarantee you'll love them!

C: 多亏你的帮助，我们暂时保住了「惊梦酒吧」，但这样终究不是长久之计。
E: Thanks to you, we managed to hold onto the Dreamjolt Hostelry, but this is not a long-term fix.

C: 在未来…我希望能让更多人接纳它们，让怪物们能在外面的社会里生活。梦境酒店实在太过狭隘，它们都很想见识更大的世界。
E: In the future... I hope more people will learn to accept monsters and allow them to enter society. The hotel in the dreamscape is just too small, they long to see the big, wide world.

C: 钢琴家做出「请」的手势，并未有过多言语。
E: Without speaking much, the pianist gives you a gentle gesture.

C: 我们很喜欢这里——我的小先生，他很久没有这样开心过了。
E: We love this place. My dear master hasn't been so happy in quite some time.

C: 但这丝毫没法动摇我对自动乐团的质疑！
E: But that doesn't mean I won't criticize automated orchestras!

C: 属于我的那个她，我早已遇见，可是…她如今在哪呢？
E: My true love, I met her long ago, but... Where is she now?

C: 那里不存在悲愁、不存在腐朽，那里繁星汇集、钟灵毓秀……
E: Neither sorrow nor decadence exist there. Myriad stars gather there, where talents and their brethren congregate...

C: 没事，问题不大，观众们听不出来…但你是艺术家，别辜负了你的琴和作品。
E: It's cool. No big deal. The audience won't be able to tell... But you're an artist, so you shouldn't be letting down your instrument and your work.

C: 哼，那你就走吧。冷漠不过是弱者用来自保的借口罢了。
E: Hmph, then go. Apathy is just an excuse the weak use to protect themselves.

C: 要说这份工作最让人神清气爽的地方，大概就是很能满足人的掌控欲了吧——只要我想，我可以依照个人心情随意调度现场的每一个人、每一件道具！
E: If you ask me, the best thing about this job is how it satisfies a person's inner control freak: I can rearrange any actor or any prop on set however I want just based on how I'm feeling!

C: 你为什么会在流梦礁？
E: Why are you here in the Dreamflux Reef?

C: 广告牌？「黄金的时刻」追着人跑的那种？
E: A billboard? Like the ones in the Golden Hour that chase after people?

C: 这样倒也没什么不好…至少我还挺满意的。毕竟「林子大了什么鸟都有」…我不擅长处理太复杂的人际关系，只想好好创作，那种麻烦事还是算了吧。
E: There is nothing wrong with this... I'm quite satisfied, at least. After all, "the wider the net you cast, the more fish you catch..." I'm not great at dealing with complex social interactions. I just want to create, and avoid trouble as much as possible.

C: 话说回来，这乱子之所以能顺利解决，肯定少不了你们星穹列车的一份功劳吧？
E: Come to think of it, this whole situation being resolved so smoothly must have been considerable thanks to your Crew, right?

C: 在外面漂泊这么久…我也有些想念家乡了。
E: Having roamed the outside world for all this time... I'm starting to miss home, too.

C: 平时有遇到什么难题吗？
E: Are there any troubles you've faced along the way?

C: 我计划在接下来的日子里，与其他退休的老师一起，为孩子们开设更加系统的课程。
E: In the coming future, I plan to work with other retired teachers to put together a more systematic syllabus.

C: …也为了她的心愿。
E: ...And for her wish.

C: 还好没出大事，您下次要小心了。
E: Luckily nothing serious happened. You should be careful next time.

C: 渐渐地，这里变得热闹起来：我们为人们演奏，用赚取的佣金进行日常的养护…每一天都充实而忙碌。大家的音符中充满欢乐，人们也常因此而获得快乐。
E: Gradually, things started to grow more lively here: We'd play for people and put the money we earned toward everyday maintenance... every day was a full and busy one. Everybody's music was brimming with joy in every note, and people got to feel a lot of joy as they listened.

C: 听懂了吗？懂了就出发吧。
E: You got that? You should get going if you have.

C: 嘿呀——让我想想，最近<color=#dbc291ff>赛场上来了只大猩猩</color>，把所有对手都打得落花流水，那个爱看戏的小家伙绝对不会错过啾。
E: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: A <color=#dbc291ff>huge gorilla has recently arrived at the racetrack</color> and demolished all its opponents. That fanfare-loving fella will definitely not miss this, tweet.

C: 小鸟的眼神里似乎凭空多了一丝委屈，让你不忍心再否定下去。
E: The bird's eyes show a sliver of hurt out of nowhere — even you're hard-pressed to deny it.

C: 作为猫头鹰老师的学生，无论是优雅的蓝调街，还是舒心的大花园，您在小镇上看到的大多数美好的事物，都是人家的手笔啾。
E: As Professor Owl's student, whether it's the elegant Blues Street, or the soothing garden, all the beautiful things you see in town are of my design, tweet.

C: 石头老板手下的那些坏蛋，总说剧场是它们的祖先建的，人家才不信啾！
E: Boss Stone and his minions keep insisting that their ancestors built the theater first. I say poppycock to that, tweet!

C: 让我想想…对了，<color=#dbc291ff>那个方方正正的房间，里面的石头都又大又整齐</color>，有个热爱建筑的孩子，可能还在那里学习啾～
E: Let me think... Oh, that's right, <color=#dbc291ff>in that square room with its big and immaculate stones</color>, there's a child who's passionate about architecture, likely still trying to learn something there, tweet!

C: 好啾，那些淘气的孩子就麻烦客官您多留心了啾～
E: Okay, I'll leave those troublesome kids to you, tweet!

C: 在匹诺康尼还是监狱之时，有一位叫做「苏萨先生」的大善人。他高超的厨艺本能在宇宙中最豪华的餐厅工作，但却选择来到了边陲监狱中救赎犯人的身体与心灵。
E: When Penacony was still a prison, there was a very kind man named Mr. Sousa. He could have worked in the most luxurious restaurant in the universe with his unmatched culinary skill, but he chose to salvage the shells and souls of prisoners in this frontier prison.

C: 自从苏乐达成为匹诺康尼的国民饮料之后，它的销量一直止步不前，这对一个企业来说是十分致命的危险。
E: Ever since SoulGlad became the national drink of Penacony, its sales have been climbing higher than ever. This is something that could ironically prove fatal to a company.

C: 我想看看你的玩具。
E: I wish to take a look at your toys.

C: 那积木只有三块，连一座塔都搭不起来，但那三块积木是我最好的朋友，是我的救星……
E: There are only three of such building blocks. Even stacked, they can't make a tower, but those three blocks are my best friends, and my salvation...

C: 听上去有些遗憾。
E: I sense a tinge of regret in your words.

C: 但我已经不会感到沮丧了。相反，我目睹了一些花朵、一些美好的东西从遗憾的裂缝里生长了出来。
E: But I'm not depressed anymore. In contrast, from the crevices of regret, I've seen some flowers and other beautiful things grow.

C: …陌生的面孔？
E: ...A stranger's visage?

C: 按他自己的说法，他一直在星间流浪，寻找着「希望」，至于方法……
E: Per his own account, he had been drifting between the stars in search for "hope." As for exactly how he was doing so...

C: 没事了。
E: That's all.

C: 那真是位尊贵的客人，若非那时我仍然是首席大厨，恐怕根本没有接待他的资格。
E: He was truly a distinguished guest. I wouldn't have had the opportunity to serve him if I wasn't still executive chef at the time.

C: 并且，她所拥有的财富，也足以支持她悠哉游哉地畅游星间。
E: Her wealth also granted her the ability to wander among the stars at her leisure.

C: 多加五个零，对这众生来说，您会和神一样，完全像是另一个物种。
E: Add five more zeros, and you might as well be a god to all living beings, existing on a different plane.

C: 我顺着这条线查了下去…这幕后黑手真狠，为了确保能用谋杀罪顶替走私罪，直接抹除了这种保护动物的存在。
E: I investigated the lead... The person behind it was utterly merciless. To flip smuggling into murder charges, they just flat-out pretended this protected animal doesn't exist.

C: 嗯…确实是美食，你很有品味，说着口水都要滴下来了。
E: Hmm, it does sound heavenly. Your taste is excellent. It's got me salivating.

C: 啊…您…您好……
E: Ah... H—Hello...

C: 哈哈，我就喜欢和你这样的聪明人聊天。
E: Haha, I really enjoy chatting with smart folks like you.

C: 你的名字是有什么说法吗？
E: Is there any meaning to your name?

C: 而且我只是个小小的零食工！跟我抱怨有用的话，我还会在这里当售货员吗？！
E: Plus, I'm just a lowly Snack Vendor! If complaining to me worked, would I still be working as a salesperson here!?

C: …感觉我活得真窝囊，人到中年，一事无成……
E: ...I feel like an utter loser in life. I've lived half of my life without accomplishing anything...

C: 好的，请你稍等，我就吩咐人去改。
E: Certainly. One moment, please. I'll have someone change it right this instant.

C: 很高兴见到您，船长。在这里，没有生物是完美的，我也不例外。但每一次航行都是一次成长的机会，我相信在您的带领下，我们会变得更好——我以能成为您的大副为荣。
E: Nice to meet you, Captain. You'll find that none of the lifeforms here are perfect, myself included. Nevertheless, every flight is an opportunity to grow. With you leading us, I'm sure we'll only keep improving and getting better. I'm honored to be your first mate.

C: 好的。
E: Understood.

C: 命名为「开拓之尾号」，尾翼配以星穹列车列车长帕姆模样的涂装。
E: The Trailblaze's Stern features an insignia designed after Pom-Pom, the conductor of the Astral Express, on the tail of the airship.

C: 命名为「塔塔洛夫号」，尾翼配以垃圾之王塔塔洛夫模样的涂装。
E: The Tatalov features an insignia designed after Tatalov the Garbage King on the tail of the airship.

C: 我侍奉家族，螺丝咕姆先生是匹诺康尼的永久贵宾。逻辑：我同样为螺丝咕姆先生服务。
E: I serve The Family. Mr. Screwllum is a Permanent VIP of Penacony. Logic: I also serve Mr. Screwllum.

C: 呵，我的人脉好些都深居幕后，恐怕报出名字来你也不见得认识。但既然你诚心发问，我就勉为其难地举个你肯定知道的例子。
E: Hah, many of my business associates are bigwigs who work behind the scenes, so people you're not likely to have heard of. But since you're interested, I guess I can name someone you're sure to know?

C: 首先，你需要一辆出色的球笼车。格拉克斯杯的赛道复杂多变，球笼车需要在多种类型的赛道稳定发挥性能。论造车，没有人拼得过筑梦学院那群学霸们。
E: First, you need an outstanding Spheroid. The tracks for the Glaux Cup are complex and varied, Spheroids need to be able to perform reliably on all kinds of tracks. When it comes to Spheroid construction, no one can compete with the Dreamweaver Academy bookworms.

C: 不过，对筑梦的热情，以及对相关课程的掌握，我有自信不会输给任何人。
E: But I'm confident that my passion for dreamweaving and my grasp of the curriculum are unmatched!

C: 就是这样的人…在我眼中格外面目可憎……
E: People like this... I find them especially loathsome...

C: 就是广告带来的恶评不太好…哎，这点我倒是能接受……
E: The poor reviews from the advertisements aren't great... *sigh* But I can accept that...

C: 好的。要是经理还有事，可以随时找我。
E: Okay. Do look for me if you need anything.

C: 学习成绩有提高吗？
E: Did her results get better?

C: 质问：你如何看待现今的学院规划？
E: Query: How do you view the current academy plan?

C: 我觉得这种销售策略实在有点缺德，虽然赚钱…但是现在我想明白了，就赚点生活费，这里的主顾又都是同学，没必要这么下作。
E: I do feel this sales strategy is unethical, although it does make money... But now I've thought it through. It's just to earn a bit of living expenses. The main customers here are all my schoolmates, and there's no need to be so despicable.

C: 还是说，我就应该在老家的培训学校学门技术，干点普通的基础工作……
E: Or maybe I should have just learned a skill at the trade school back home and just worked a basic job...

C: 醒醒，你还要补考呢。
E: Wake up. You still need to do the make-up exam.

C: 我、我们能不聊这个话题吗…我不想在校庆期间再想学习的问题了……
E: C—Can we not talk about this... I don't want to think about my results during the school anniversary...

C: 啊…是关于「年鉴」的事…{NICKNAME}同学，可、可以帮我在这一页写几句留言吗？
E: Ah... It's about the yearbook... {NICKNAME}, c—can you write a message for me on this page?

C: 嗯…我的未来，会是什么样子的呢……
E: Hmm... What will my future look like...

C: 学校也太过分了，下半学期的课程居然这么多…虽然很多是我挂科要补课的内容，但这样就没法继续开摊了。
E: The school's gone too far. There are so many lessons for the second half of the semester... Though they are all remedial lessons for subjects I failed in — I won't be able to open my stall at this rate.

C: 再见，亲爱的无名客。愿您旅途顺遂。
E: Goodbye, dear Nameless. Have a safe voyage.

C: 不过…也许是闲来无事，看着偌大的车厢，总有种想要整理一番的冲动……
E: Yet... as I look at the spacious car, I feel a strong urge to organize it that likely stems from boredom...

C: 的确，盛会之星大名鼎鼎，但在家族不计其数的领地中，也只称得上是一处「酒店」。
E: The Planet of Festivities is certainly well-known, but among The Family's many territories, it is merely a "hotel" at best.

C: 或许这小小的意外，也是某种喻示吧。
E: Perhaps this is some sort of sign.

C: 向你致意，一如往常。
E: My best regards, as always.

C: 有时候，一个地方看着能过去，摸着能过去，但确实就是过不去。
E: Sometimes, you look at a place and think you can pass through. Sometimes, it'll even feel like you can, but in reality, you can't.

C: 巨型梦境迷钟，设计之初曾具备星际飞行与变形合体的功能，后来被甲方砍掉了。
E: A giant Dream Ticker. Its original design was made with interstellar flight and transformation functions in mind, but the client scaled down later.

C: 真想亲眼见见船长！
E: I really want to meet the captain in person!

C: 或许是某种新人到岗的热情，又或许是他们对这份职业的高度自律，在你任命完大副后，大副立刻向你提交了一份简略的工作报告。
E: Perhaps due to their enthusiasm to welcome a newcomer to the post, or the high level of professionalism they have for their work, immediately after you appointed the first mate, he submits a brief work report to you.

C: 石板与卷轴在一隅堆积成山，却未染上一丝灰尘。
E: Slates and scrolls are piled high in the corner, yet not a speck of dust can be found on them.

C: 可这对智械并不公平。
E: But that's not fair to Intellitrons.

C: 在梦里遇到了喜欢的人。
E: I met someone I like in the dream.

C: 要是我在梦里跑步。
E: If I were to run in my dream.

C: 身体不自觉就扭起来了！
E: My body is moving instinctively!

C: 你懂艺术吗？
E: Do even you get art?

C: 我会帮您向家族反映的。
E: I'll help you pass this feedback to The Family.

C: 交情深，一口闷！
E: Bottoms up! To our friendship!

C: 你说有阿基维利的梦？
E: Wait, they have Akivili's dream?

C: 嘘，那个是机密。
E: Shh, keep it on the down-low.

C: 谢谢你…你真好！
E: Thank you... You are so kind!

C: 还可以和动漫人物握手~
E: You can even shake hands with cartoon characters~

C: 黑暗无所遁形！
E: Darkness cannot escape!

C: 您有权拒绝，不过那样我们会感到深深的遗憾。
E: You have the right to refuse, but we would be extremely regretful should that be your decision.

C: 说得像你没参赛就喝得完一样…
E: As if you could finish your supply even without participating...

C: 经历了那么多磨难，好不容易才走到决赛舞台，那位红色的骑士真是太可惜了…光是听他讲那些故事，都让我哭得稀里哗啦的。
E: After all the trials and tribulations to make it to the finals, it's a shame for the Red Knight... Just listening to his stories made me bawl.

C: 这次的盛会巨星肯定是她！
E: I bet she'll be the superstar of this contest!

C: 这称号不挺帅的吗？
E: What, I think this moniker's pretty cool, no?

C: 现在，我们就在海选大厅的现场。
E: Now, we're live in the audition lobby.

C: 为选手带来不一样的体验…
E: To bring the contestants an experience never seen before...

C: 你们从哪儿招的人，这小伙子弹得挺好的。
E: Where'd you get this guy? This youngster's a pretty good musician.

C: 哎，我知道这事儿，确实影响蛮大的。算了算了，珍品多等等也是应该的……
E: *sigh* I know about this. It's actually quite a big deal. Forget it. Forget it. Treasures are worth the wait...

C: 醒醒，你就是在做梦。
E: Wake up. You're dreaming.

C: 我是皮皮西，不是未成年！
E: I'm a Pepeshi, not a minor!

C: 我现在跑还来得及吗？
E: Is it too late for me to run?

C: 知更鸟小姐，谐乐学院的荣誉校友，也是一位客座教师——偌大的匹诺康尼，还有谁能不喜欢知更鸟小姐呢？
E: Miss Robin, honored alumnus of Charmony Academy, also a guest lecturer — who doesn't love her on Penacony?

C: 你组建的文学社至此总算完成了它的使命，成为了新世界最初的那块垫脚石。
E: The literature club you formed formally concludes its duty at this moment, becoming the first stepping stone for a new world.

C: 这才是乐队的初衷，不是吗？不用时刻担心会有人来结束这美好的一切。
E: This is what a band should be, no? No need to be mired in constant worry that someone is here to end this beautiful lifestyle.
...Huo... huo...

C: 你玩得太久，手指都起了褶皱。
E: You've played for so long that your fingers have wrinkled.

C: 你询问它今日运势，它却沉默不语。大概因为这只是一个摆件吧。
E: You ask it about fortune for today, but it remains mute. Perhaps because it's just a decorative item.

C: 你仔细打量这艘属于自己的船，船身的每道弧度在你眼里都是如此完美。
E: You carefully examine your personal ship. Every curve of the hull appears perfect in your eyes.

C: 摸摸它。
E: Pet it.

C: 哼，哼哼~
E: Oink, oink oink~

C: 哼，哼哼！
E: Oink, oink oink!

C: 大地兽欧斐摩斯
E: Let's talk about Euphemus.

C: 唉，那一趟可实在点背，四个那么大的箱子，我还以为肯定装了什么金银财宝——结果价值还抵不过路途上的开销。
E: Yeah, that was an unlucky trip, I admit. I was hoping for some real treasures in those crates, but those rubber ducks barely covered our costs.

C: 卡斯托尔和波鲁克斯是两头年轻的大地兽，卡斯托尔比波鲁克斯稍稍年长一点，它们都还不曾经历那些考验生死的冒险。
E: Castor and Pollux are a pair of young dromases. Castor is a bit older than Pollux, and neither has faced any life-or-death adventures yet.

C: 说到这儿，我还有另一件头疼事——近期顾客们越来越爱看轻快的读物，情节爽快的故事、<unbreak>30</unbreak>秒速读的搞笑小石板都成了畅销品。
E: Speaking of that, there's a new challenge for me. Nowadays, customers prefer lighter fiction. Quick-paced plots, humorous tales, and mini comedy tablets featuring <unbreak>30</unbreak>-second stories are flying off the shelves.

C: 桌上的拼盘被愤怒的食客挥手砸在地上，发出「啪」的一声脆响——
E: In a fit of rage, the customer hurls his hand, sending the plate on the table crashing to the floor with a loud pop.

C: 接下来是主菜——请尝尝左边的肉排，它选用新鲜的岩羊肋排肉，配上秘制酱料炭烤而成，无论在营养还是口感层面都能满足大部分人群。
E: Next, please savor the main course — the juicy rocklamb rib meat on the left, charcoal-grilled in my secret sauce, a balance of nutrition and flavor.

C: 都说了，{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}的神威持续不了多久——换句话说，你可以下次问其他的问题。
E: I told you, the power of {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#} won't last long — so you may save other questions for the next time.

C: 可问题是下次喝了盲盒也不一定是这个效果啊？
E: But the Blind Box might not have the same effect next time, right?

C: 苹果是奥赫玛——或者说整个翁法罗斯——最常见的水果，而你手中这个是经过多次改良的品种，营养更加丰富，保鲜时间也更长。
E: Apples are the most common fruit here in Okhema — perhaps in all of Amphoreus. This apple in your hand is from a cultivar that has been genetically improved multiple times, bred to be extra nutritious and stay fresh for longer.

C: 我来找你聊聊天。
E: I'm here for a little chat.

C: 从预先知道结果、但完全不知道应该怎样努力的角度来说，黄金裔们已经做得很好了。有些人对他们的苛责才是毫无道理。
E: Given that the Chrysos Heirs know the outcome but have no idea about how to get there, I think they are already doing a very good job. Some of the criticism they face is just plain unfair.

C: 我编了一套石板上的程序，设计算法寻找可以密铺镶嵌的五边形马赛克，总共找出来了<unbreak>15</unbreak>种——只可惜有建筑师后来和我说，五边形的地砖在生产成本上还是有点太超前了。
E: In the end, I designed a program on the tablet and wrote algorithms to find pentagon-shaped mosaics that could cover the floor smoothly, and I discovered <unbreak>15</unbreak> different patterns. But when I mentioned the idea to an architect, they warned me that mass-producing pentagonal tiles might be too costly.

C: 过了一阵……
E: Later...

C: 凡人终生难遇其一，如是精彩纷呈；而你——
E: A mortal may encounter such brilliance only once in a lifetime. Yet you...

C: 超可爱的缇宝、缇宁、缇安。
E: The adorable Tribbie, Trinnon, and Trianne.

C: 等他从创世涡心中凯旋，我们也能离我们的夙愿更进一步。
E: When he returns triumphant from the Vortex of Genesis, we will be one step closer to our long-held wish.

C: …抱歉，我的措辞有误，那里曾是离奥赫玛最近的度假胜地，嗯…*曾是*。
E: ...Sorry, I should rephrase that. It was the closest scenic holiday spot to Okhema... Was.

C: 我记得我举起弓箭，瞄准了最大的那只。我祈求艾格勒赐予我力量和准头。然后...然后箭矢飞出去了，正中目标！
E: I remember I raised my bow and hit the biggest one. I prayed to Aquila to give me strength and accuracy. Then... Then I fired my arrow and got a direct hit!

C: 我没什么想占卜的。
E: I don't need my fortune told.

C: …啧，行吧行吧，给你就给你。
E: ...Tch. Okay, fine. Take this.

C: 哦。烟火气也是分很多种的，奥赫玛的烟火气又尤其与众不同。
E: Mmm. There's a variety of warmth to life, and Okhema's is particularly unique.

C: …是不是感觉自己都有点饿了？但遗憾的是，咱真正回家的时候，要么就是谁因为什么不开心的事吊着臭脸，要么就是大家不知道都出去做什么了、空空荡荡一个人都没有。
E: ...Aren't you starting to feel a little hungry? Unfortunately, when we get back to our homes, someone is unhappy about something or other, or everyone's off out of the house doing something else, leaving it empty.

C: 就别为难人家啦。
E: Don't put people out.

C: 有什么需要吗？客人。
E: Is there something I can help you with, dear customer?

C: 动手前，你花了一刹那思考，说不定阿格莱雅之后会很啰嗦…要不，饶他们一命算了。
E: Before you make your move, you spend a few seconds in reflection. There's a chance that Aglaea might have something to say about this... Maybe you should let them go with their lives this time...?

C: 我怕死，快教我。
E: I am scared of death. Please teach me!

C: 好样的，兄弟，精神点！
E: You go, bro! Stay sharp!

C: 武器…很特殊，那位丹恒阁下。
E: Very special, his weapon is, that Lord Dan Heng.

C: 我为黄金裔，打造好的武器。也只能从武器说起，我的了解。
E: Good weapons, I craft for Chrysos Heirs. From weapons, my knowledge begins.

C: 你不禁勾起了嘴角……
E: You instinctively curl the corners of your lips...

C: 今天有些忙，就聊到这吧。
E: I'm busy today, so let's put a pin in things there.

C: 那就好。我始终对你有些愧疚，要是能帮上忙，我的心里也会好受些。
E: That's good to hear. I've always felt somewhat guilty toward you, so knowing I've been able to help eases my mind.

C: 雅努斯，不好。密径、传信石版，都太小，吉奥里亚的子民难用。
E: Janus unsuitable. Paths, teleslates too small, for Georios' kin.

C: 就算在覆灭前，城邦的面积与人口也在不断发生变化，你童年时见到的城邦，和青年时见到的必然不同。既然如此，你凭什么断定它就是阿卡索斯？
E: Even before its destruction, the area and population of the city were undergoing constant change. The city that you saw as a child was undoubtedly different to the one you saw as a youth. If that is the case, how can you conclude that it was indeed Icatus?

C: 秉着简洁的原则，我认为——<color=#dbc291ff>「阿卡索斯」并不存在</color>，能确定的只是*有个城邦*，仅此而已。
E: Based on the principle of simplicity, I think that... "Icatus" does not in fact exist, and all we can be certain of is that there is a city.

C: 原来如此，我还以为你也是来欣赏壁画的呢。
E: I see. I thought you were here to appreciate the mural.

C: 我还有事，先走了。
E: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

C: 嘴上嚷嚷着翁法罗斯人传承浴场文化义不容辞，我看你们呀，只是想趁机秀出你们的健身成果，享受旁人的垂涎。
E: You keep going on about how the people of Amphoreus are duty-bound to uphold the heritage of bath culture, but I see through you all — you just want to show off your fitness gains and enjoy the envy of others.

C: 等这些哥们上战场就知道了，肌肉需要消耗更多的能量，没有脂肪储备的身材不抗寒、不耐打、不扛饿，一打一个不吱声。
E: Once these guys get to the battlefield, they'll understand. Muscles consume more energy, and without fat reserves, the body can't withstand the cold, can't take hits, and can't go without food. One hit and they'll go down without a sound.

C: 你有点像某个熟人。
E: You remind me of someone familiar.

C: 是的，我就是刻法勒。
E: Yes, I am Kephale.

C: 至于创作的主题——三成泰坦传说，两成黄金裔的故事，一成日常生活，剩下的全是{RUBY_B#夜景}欧洛尼斯的帷幕{RUBY_E#}，特适合在罐子上画一周全景。
E: As for themes — I've done <unbreak>30%</unbreak> Titan legends, <unbreak>20%</unbreak> stories of Chrysos Heirs, <unbreak>10%</unbreak> daily life, and the rest are all {RUBY_B#night scenes} the curtains of Oronyx{RUBY_E#}, which is perfect for painting a circular view on a jar.

C: 他们喜欢，我心态稳定，双赢。
E: They like it, and I stay calm. It's a win-win.

C: 下次再聊吧。
E: Let's catch up another time.

C: 其次是医典、哲章、史记…同类书目彼此间多有矛盾之处，借阅的人几乎都是为了给辩论添点物证才会借下。
E: Next are the medical compendiums, philosophical texts, and historical records... there are many contradictions between these similar books, and most people borrow them to add evidence for their debates.

C: 我的老朋友们担心之后的营生，我嘛，运气好些，利衡无忧，就担心起这门学问失传，一合计，把浴场的闲置区域改造了一番，成了个谁都能用的小教学室。
E: My old friends were worried about their future livelihoods, but I, being luckier and with a steady balance of wealth, only worried about this knowledge being lost. So, after some discussion, we decided to repurpose an unused area of the baths into a small classroom that anyone could use.

C: 但是…嗯…应该没关系，我来吧。
E: But... Hmm... It should be fine. Let me try.

C: …愉快么。这可难说，我是在为享受沐浴时光的客人演奏，若是音色太过沉郁，未免有些不识趣了吧。说到底，这乐声只能做消遣用，可算不得什么艺术。
E: ...Joy, was it? That's hard to say. I play for the guests enjoying their baths. If my music was too melancholic, it might sour some of their moods. After all, this music is merely for entertainment, unworthy to be called true art.

C: 空腹会让你的战斗力下降七成，务必吃饱了再上战场。
E: An empty stomach will reduce your combat effectiveness. You have to take to the field on a full belly.

C: 士兵毕竟不是来餐馆点菜的顾客，有时也不能太顺着他们，对吧？
E: I mean, soldiers aren't customers ordering food at a diner, so I can't always cater to them, can I?

C: 至于表现…这么跟你说吧，悬锋城的士兵总共分为三种人：鲁莽者、勇敢者和犹豫者，而我是第四种——怯懦者。
E: As for how I did... I'll just break it down for you — there are three kinds of soldiers in Castrum Kremnos: The brash, the brave, and the balky. And I'm the fourth kind: The craven.

C: 先聊这些吧。
E: Let's end our chat here.

C: 或许我也该像丹恒那样，搞几本书来看。
E: Perhaps I should pick up some books and read, just like Dan Heng did.

C: 好吧，你的坚定意志取得了胜利。不过，这样的战斗对你来说有什么好处呢？
E: Alright, your iron will wins this round. But does winning a battle like this actually benefit you in any way?

C: 查看《离别纪念》
E: View Farewell Memorial

C: 只可惜在如今的年代，人们身处黑潮包围之中，已经绝无可能再抵达那里…即使想效法古人，也只能象征性地周游世界，无法穿过黑潮去世界真正的尽头朝圣。
E: Unfortunately, in modern times, people are completely encircled by the black tide and can no longer make this journey... Even if they wish to do as the ancients did, they can only make a symbolic journey around the world. They cannot pass through the black tide to pilgrimage to the true end of the world.

C: 在世界诞生之前，雅努斯就已经醒来：那时它落脚的混沌之地，就是如今雅努萨波利斯的前身。
E: Janus awakened before the world had even been born: The chaotic land where they stood back then was the predecessor of the Janusopolis that we know today.

C: 这珍宝像进了你私人的腰包。
E: I saw you stuff my treasure into your pocket.

C: 啊？没有没有，我不是神殿的工作人员…当然是免费啦！
E: Ah? No, I don't work for the temple... It's certainly free!

C: 你有遇到什么有趣的人吗？
E: Have you met any interesting people here?

C: …唉。命运有时候就是这么蛮不讲理。
E: ...Ah, such is unreasonable fate.

C: 至于她的身世…不如直接找她当面询问吧？呵…她可不是个擅长拒绝的孩子啊。
E: As for her backstory... why not ask her directly? Heh... She's not the kind to easily refuse others.

C: 想和缇宝聊聊天！
E: I want to chat with Tribbie!

C: 如今就连缇安，仅存的三者之一，也耗尽了力量。今后，门匠的职责将由缇宝继续履行。她已下定了决心，*她们*已下定了决心。
E: Now, even Trianne, one of the last three, has exhausted her strength. From now on, the duty of the gatekeeper will be carried on by Tribbie. She has made her decision — they have made their decision.

C: 我想着你和我一样都是年轻人，你可能像我一样对政治没那么感兴趣…你不知道，这里尽是满嘴政治的老人，我都快闷死了。也许我们能聊得来……
E: Because, honestly, I think you're like me: Young and uninterested in politics. You wouldn't believe how suffocating it is, being surrounded by people who talk about nothing else. I thought maybe we could relate...

C: 我本来以为我能认识下年轻的同事，但是吧，我的上司让我别去打招呼，什么也别问，我这人也听话，就真的什么也没问。
E: I thought it'd be a chance to get to know a fellow junior colleague, but my superior told me not to approach them, not to ask anything, and to just mind my own business. Being the obedient type, I did exactly that.

C: 算起来那也是很多年前了…那时候我自觉年少成名，和志同道合的几个演员朋友一起，凑了点路费就打算开启一场环游整个世界的巡回演出。
E: It happened many years ago... Back then, I thought I was a rising star. A few actor friends and I, driven by shared ambition, scraped together enough money to embark on a grand tour around the world.

C: 一位诗人毕生追求着新的诗歌，直至最后——「我不要庭院的医者宣告我的死亡，我要自己宣告这自由。」
E: A poet spent her life pursuing new verses, till the very end. "I seek no healer in the Courtyard to declare my demise, for I shall proclaim this freedom with my own cries."

C: 彼时黑潮压境，家族直系恐怕只剩我一人了。举城迁来奥赫玛时，我护住一人，又被黄金裔阁下所救，那时我才理解，何为「贵族的荣耀」。
E: With the black tide at our doorsteps, I'm afraid I am the only surviving heir of my family. It was during the great migration to Okhema, when I sheltered another and was in turn rescued by the Chrysos Heir, that I finally understood the meaning of a noble's honor.

C: 放肆！你是与那些渎神者为伍吗？竟敢质疑泰坦了？
E: How dare you! Do you conspire with those blasphemers? How dare you question the Titans!

C: 不行了，我一看谜语就头疼。既然贵客已经拿到金织了，我还是继续做个迷糊衣匠吧。
E: No. I can't do it. Riddles give me a headache. Since you've already obtained the golden weave, I'll just continue being the confused garmentmaker.

C: 各位，呆在这里。安全，这里。
E: Everyone, stay here. Safe, here.

C: 心平气和，共度难关！
E: Stay calm and composed, and we will overcome this together!

C: 流亡者啊，去再造幸福的庭园吧\n别忘记笑语、欢宴与佳酿
E: O exiles, go rebuild a garden of happiness.\nDon't forget the laughter, the feasts, and the fine wine.

C: 嚯。你像来古士老师一样一言不发。
E: Ah, you're giving me the silent treatment — just like Mr. Lygus would.

C: 也只好且行且看啦。
E: I suppose we'll just have to wait and see.

C: 在天灾降临之时，我曾向{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}祈祷，我献上最珍贵的收藏作为祭品，可换来的…只有突然倒塌的神像与它的沉默。
E: When the calamity struck, I prayed to {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, offering my most precious collections as sacrifices, but all I got in return... was a crumbling statue and silence.

C: 老先生，不必如此多礼。
E: Sir, there's no need for that.

C: 女孩试着用手势与你「交谈」，虽然你不懂手语，不过迷迷接替了翻译的职责——
E: The girl tries to communicate with you through gestures. You don't understand sign language, but Mem steps in to translate —

C: 但，连永不熄灭的黎明机器都撑不住了，我看这就是翁法罗斯的终点了……
E: But even the ever-burning Dawn Device can't hold up anymore. I think this is the end of Amphoreus...

C: <color=#dbc291ff>「逐火的脚下本无是无物。但若求助于高天，无物中亦可窥见前路。」</color>
E: <color=#dbc291ff>"The Flame-Chase treads on nothingness. But those who seek aid from the sky may glimpse a path in the void."</color>

C: 该换更大的锅了迷！
E: Time to get a bigger pot, memi!

C: 玩会再出发迷！
E: One more round of play, then we head out, memi!

C: 谁叫你是我的好孩子呢。从小到大，你想做什么，抬抬手我就知道啦。
E: You've always been easy to read, my sweet child. Since you were little, I just had to glance your way to know what's on your mind.

C: 不会的，妈。我一定不会让大家失望…也不会让你和老爸失望。
E: I won't, mom. I won't let anyone down... not them, not you, and not Dad.

C: 这里吗？那我来介绍下吧。我把村里的所有孩子按照年龄分成了三个班，每个班上课的时间，教的课程都各不相同。
E: Oh? Then let me give you a quick tour. I've divided all the village kids into three classes by age. Each group has different lessons and schedules.

C: 这个音符是棕黄色的，像故事里说的秋收的田野。
E: This note is brownish-gold, like an autumn harvest field in the tales.

C: 生活也要过得一丝不苟，马马虎虎可不行迷！
E: That's how life should be lived too — steady and precise. No slacking, mem!

C: 感谢你所做的贡献，贵客。如此多的织者嘤鸣，令我想起众多预言中的一则。
E: Thank you for your dedicated work, my esteemed guest. The symphony of weaver chirps reminds me of a special prophecy.

C: 行啊，贵客，你想聊些什么呢？
E: Sure, honored guest. What would you like to talk about?

C: 嗯…一杯、两杯…一桶、两桶…继续喝，您也喝——
E: Yep... One mug, two mugs... one keg, two kegs... Keep going... You too...

C: 本狮让他细说，他说在奥赫玛那时，醉后建了个辩论场，想和公民们探讨凯撒大人的伟大之处，发了很多长篇大论。
E: I prompted him for more details, and he talked about how he had set up a Forum in Okhema when he was drunk. He wanted to discuss the eminence of the Great Imperator with the citizens, and posted many long essays.

C: 来都来了，和我一起看看彩虹吧嘤~
E: You've come all the way here, let's admire the rainbow together, eep~

C: 我一直待在这里，等待水流把我带往未知的终点……
E: I'll continue to stay here and wait for the water currents to bring me to an unknown destination...

C: 当茧褪去时，唯余一块金丝织成的锦缎漂浮在空中。衣匠伸手一挥，它便分作两半，其中一半飞进了你的手里……
E: When the cocoon fades, only a piece of golden brocade remains floating in the air. The Garmentmaker waves a hand, and it splits in two, with one half flying into your grasp...

C: 前半句指的应该是雅努斯迷津？至于这后半句…至少答案应该是一个数字。
E: The first part likely refers to the Janus' Maze? As for the second... well, the answer's probably a number, at least.

C: 唉，没见你多能赚钱，败家倒是一把好手！
E: *sigh* You're no good at earning money, but you sure know how to spend it!

C: 我拿到情报了，想成为黄金裔不难。
E: I've got the intel. It's not hard to become a Chrysos Heir.

C: 愿你们迎来安详。
E: May you embrace peace.

C: 正好被我现在的上司看到，他就把我招进来了。
E: And my current boss just so happened to see that, and hired me on the spot.

C: 嗯。…记得，提醒客人。
E: Okay... Remember, do remind the customer.

C: 非也！议题并非探讨概念前后，而是名词的生辰。而黄金裔的故事被传颂前，根本无人知晓究竟「何为金血」。
E: On the contrary! We're not talking about the before or after of the concept, but the day the words were created. Before the Chrysos Heirs' story was sung in paeans, no one knew what "golden blood" was.

C: 兄弟，你注意到没，咱们换班的间隔越来越长了。
E: Say, bro, you ever noticed that the time between shifts has been getting longer and longer...

C: 阿米库斯，够了，快住手，别再乱亲那头大地兽了，你没看到它都快急眼了吗？
E: Amycus, that's enough! Stop that, stop kissing that dromas. Can't you see it's getting angry?

C: …啊这，这…这不太好吧，这池子好歹是我们泡过的啊…我怎么下得去嘴？
E: ...Ah, th—that's... not a good idea. The pools here are where we bathe... How could I ever drink that?

C: 啧，行吧，那就全体休息一刻钟，然后再来二十组俯卧撑。
E: Pfft, fine. Take a quint, then finish twenty sets of push-ups!

C: 您这话可就说对了！别急，我们专门安排了后续的购物环节，等看完这里就过去……
E: You said it right! Don't worry, we have a special shopping session planned afterward. We'll go after we wrap up here...

C: 你们这是…对元老院不敬。啊…不对，你们是在对泰坦不敬啊……
E: You're... disrespecting the Council of Elders. Ah... No! Your disrespect is toward the Titans themselves...

C: 你就不能靠谱一回吗？
E: Can't I rely on you for once in your life?

C: 现在的袍子真不行。
E: Those modern robes won't do at all.

C: 说到吃的，最重要的肯定是外观。
E: When it comes to food, appearance is definitely what's most important.

C: 咕咕——孩子们都跟着你回来了，它们果然很信任你。
E: Hoot hoot... The children have all returned with you. They really trust you so much.

C: 我是「猫头鹰老师」，帮一群厉害的朋友们建造了美梦小镇咕。
E: I am Professor Owl. I helped a group of amazing friends build Dreamville, hoot.

C: 作为研究者，我对于珍稀物种都是平等看待的，不会有喜恶之别。
E: As a researcher, I treat every rare species equally. I don't have a preference.

C: 我有一些问题…
E: I have some questions...

C: 我和那位希拉女士是同一个科室的科员——法政科。
E: I came from the same department as Ms. Sheila, the Department of Galactic Geopolitics.

C: 你有见过那位大人吗？
E: Have you seen that lord?

C: 倒是你，看着有些面生…啊，我知道了，你是星穹列车的那伙人！
E: And you, you don't look like you are from around here... Ah, I know. You're one of those people from the Astral Express!

C: 如果有身体不适，千万别去医疗科指名我，我每天可忙着呢。
E: If you are not feeling well, do not ask for me in the Medical Department. I am very busy.

C: 啊？你问我之后怎么着了？年轻人，这听八卦可不兴一口气听完。想要知道，就请听下回分解吧！
E: What's that? You want to know what happened then? Kiddo, I can't spill all the beans in one go. Stay tuned!

C: 真不是我说，你们年轻人其实不爱听这句，但我还是得说——没有啥忘不掉的事儿，没有啥过不去的坎儿！真要是有，那就是当时没琢磨明白，当局者迷了！
E: I know young people these days hate to hear this, but nothing is truly unforgettable, and there's nothing you can't get over! If there is, it's only because you've been too lost to figure it out!

C: 老板好英明~
E: Boss is so smart!

C: 我知道，瑟希斯的奥体…已经被天火洞穿，但它会活在每一位学者的思维里！
E: I know, Cerces's divine body... has been pierced by heavenly fire... but they will live on in the minds of every scholar!

C: 爬上来不就行了？
E: Why don't you just climb up here?

C: 你没看见吗？我们之间已经隔了一层{RUBY_B#空气墙}可悲的厚障壁{RUBY_E#}了喀…
E: Can't you see? There's already a {RUBY_B#invisible wall}pathetic thick barrier{RUBY_E#} between us, crrk...

C: 哦，原来是道上的同行喀！
E: Oh, so you're a fellow practitioner, crrk!

C: 我想，找到那些若虫以后，我应该就能拿回属于我的记忆、回到我曾在的那座圣城了。
E: I believe once I find those nymphs, I'll reclaim my lost memories and return to that holy city where I once belonged.

C: 嗯…有位织者正沉醉于周遭景色中。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>低处的墙垣上</color>轻声感叹呢。
E: Hmm... There's a weaver who is lost in the scenery. The golden thread brings tidings: It's quietly sighing from the <color=#dbc291ff>low-lying platform</color>.

C: 当茧褪去时，唯余一块金丝织成的锦缎漂浮在空中。衣匠伸手一挥，它便分作两半，其中一半飞进了你的手里……
E: When the cocoon fades, only a piece of golden brocade remains floating in the air. The Garmentmaker waves a hand, and it splits in two, with one half flying into your grasp...

C: 衣匠小心接过记忆残晶，优雅地炼化。
E: The Garmentmaker carefully receives the Memory Crystal Shards and elegantly refines them.

C: 别那么谦虚嘛，你可是咱们餐厅未来的希望。怎么样，要不要来和我学做陶罐啊？
E: Don't be so modest. You're the future hope of our restaurant. What do you say, wanna learn how to make dolia with me?

C: 好久不见，{NICKNAME}！告诉你个好消息，下届「奥凯纳斯运动会」将在{RUBY_B#二月}平衡月{RUBY_E#}中旬举行，我给你在第一排留了位置！
E: Long time no see, {NICKNAME}! I've got some great news — the next "Okeanas Games" will be held in the middle of {RUBY_B#February}the Month of Balance{RUBY_E#}, and I've saved you a front-row seat!

C: 唔…我最喜欢看记着黄金世英雄故事的壁画了。我常常用木炭笔，在石砖上自己涂涂画画呢。
E: Hmm... I love looking at the murals depicting the heroes of the Era Chrysea. I often use charcoal to doodle and draw on the stone bricks myself.

C: 哈哈…这是个不可多得的好机会！
E: Haha... This is a golden opportunity!

C: 多亏了你，我们推理协会才能有重聚的一天！不过…这才团聚没多久，唉……
E: Thanks to you, our Deduction Society finally reunited! But... we've only just gotten back together, and now... *sigh*

C: 哦…那，何乐不为呢？多少利衡币？
E: Oh... well, why not? How many Balance Coins?

C: 聊呗，来食堂，聊。
E: Sure. Come diner. Chat.

C: 喔，贵客来了！怎么样，陪我们去痛饮一番？
E: Oh, look who's here! How about joining us for some drinks?

C: 呵…当真是一番有趣的开场白。
E: Heh... now that's quite an intriguing opening line.

C: 我来当模特。
E: I'll be the model.

C: 我就知道裁缝女叫我们来准没安好心，这不，又想让我们帮她打工了。
E: I knew that seamstress had something up her sleeve when she called us here. Look, she's trying to get us to work for her again.

C: 我的图书馆并未设锁，「钥匙」在立下约定的那一刻就已经递交到你手中了。
E: My library has no locks. The key was placed in your hands the moment our promise was made.

C: 和小伊卡进行蜜酿大比拼？
E: A honey brew showdown with Little Ica?

C: 期待你的其他职业道路。
E: I look forward to your future career choices.

C: 有点忘记那回事了。
E: I kinda forgot about that.

C: 光是看着绿茵茵的作物，人家就能想象出一桌佳肴的样子！
E: Just looking at those lush green crops makes me imagine a whole feast laid out on the table!

C: 哦！还有后记：「所谓神话都掺杂了后人附会，两位泰坦并没有传说中的那么相亲相爱，不过只是一点零星的合作关系……」
E: Oh! And there's a postscript: "All myths get embellished by later generations. These two Titans weren't as lovey-dovey as the legends suggest, they just had some occasional collaboration..."

C: 所谓神话都掺杂了后人附会。
E: All myths are mixed with later embellishments.

C: 哎嘿嘿，上次来玩的时候偷偷放在这的，总算被你发现了。
E: Hehe~ I secretly left it here last time I came to play. You finally found it!

C: 我们的旅途还在继续，这就够啦！后面你们俩记得多请我喝几杯奶茶就行！
E: Our journey continues, and that's all that matters! Just remember to treat me to lots of bubble tea later, you two!

C: 是啊，英雄——故事家会这样称呼他们，而我更愿称其为自由之人、充满无限可能性的人。
E: Yes, heroes. That's what storytellers call them, but I prefer to call them free spirits, people of infinite possibilities.

C: 比如「星际和平公司」：他们崇拜「存护」克里珀，却阴错阳差成了银河最大的经济体；
E: The Interastral Peace Corporation is a good example: They worship Qlipoth, the Aeon of Preservation, but somehow became the largest economic entity in the galaxy.

C: 后记不知道到哪里去找。
E: I don't know where to find the postscript.

C: …我看你这小子就是想把我气死！
E: ... Brat, you're trying to send me to an early grave, aren't you!?

C: 看不懂，嘿嘿。
E: I don't get it either. Hehe.

C: 看我的！
E: Watch this!

C: 没什么想问的了。
E: That's all questions I have.

C: 不愧是你！大矿区那件事之后，我就很欣赏你的能力……
E: As expected! Ever since the incident in the Great Mine, I knew I could always count on you...

C: 师傅倒好，在家歇得舒服…我什么时候也能退休？
E: My master's doing well. He's relaxing at home... When can I retire too?

C: 滴答，宾客，我要逃去其他梦境休假了，再见！
E: Tick-tock, dear guest, I'm going to escape to other Dreamscapes to take a break. Bye!

C: 滴、答，可恶，酒店里的那个破迷钟居然升职了，肯定是跟主管挨得近，天天忙着拍马屁！
E: Tick, tock, darn it! That stupid Ticker from the hotel got promoted! They must be close to the manager, busy sucking up to the top brass!

C: 我目睹家族掠夺走迷钟们的奖金，然后是睡眠时间。美梦是一台冰冷的机器，我们——我和你，都是微不足道的燃料。
E: I've seen The Family plunder the Tickers' rewards, and then it's time to sleep. The dream is a cold, merciless machine where we, you and I, are mere fuel for it.

C: 滴、答…好寂寞…我好寂寞……
E: Tick, tock... So lonely... I'm so lonely...

C: 我要修好你。
E: I'm trying to fix you.

C: 怎么样，{NICKNAME}？您挑战过「星芒战幕」了吗？
E: What's up, {NICKNAME}? Have you had a go at Stellar Flare?

C: 仅限于看过档案的程度，不过她之前生活的地方我倒是很熟悉。「朋克洛德」，是个很开放的赛博星球。
E: Only what I could read from the program file. I do know a bit about where she's from: Punklorde — an extremely open cyberworld.

C: 你也没做什么吧。
E: You didn't do anything.

C: 「而其中最强的一位，正在和你对话。」
E: "The most skilled of which, is currently talking to you."

C: 「我想离开那里，而星核猎手也欢迎我加入——就这么简单。」
E: "I wanted out of there, and the Stellaron Hunters were very welcoming — it's that simple."

C: 嗯，嗯…没问题，价钱没错。这幅《晨曦》就交给你了。
E: Mmm... All good. This is the right price. You can take this Ray of Dawn.

C: 嗯，嗯…没问题，价钱没错。看来那家伙还没糊涂。
E: Mmm... All good. This is the right price. Looks like he hasn't quite lost all his marbles just yet.

C: 用不了多久，你就能在综合馆最显眼的位置看到它了。这次也辛苦你了，{NICKNAME}。
E: Soon enough. This'll have pride of place in the General Hall. Well done, {NICKNAME}.

C: 这组机柜应该是操控矿用机械的终端，这个型号也已经被淘汰了。
E: These cabinets should be the terminal to control the mining gear. This model is long obsolete.

C: {NICKNAME}！你可终于来了！我正到处找你呢！
E: {NICKNAME}! You're finally here! I've been looking everywhere for you!

C: 尽管如此，母亲大人仍将我当作最亲爱的女儿——或许她是将所剩无几的温情留给了我，而非她的子民……
E: Nevertheless, mother still regarded me as her dearest daughter, perhaps leaving what little warmth she had left for me, instead of her people...

C: 我想再重新考虑一下。
E: I would like to reconsider.

C: 既然你这样言之凿凿…那就给我看看吧——如果你真有证据的话。
E: Since you sound so sure of yourself, I'll take a look at your evidence.

C: 没想到下层区是这样的风貌……
E: I had no idea the Underworld would look like this...

C: 一幅摄影作品，记录了克里珀堡中大守护者的办公室。
E: A work of photography that captures the Supreme Guardian's office in Qlipoth Fort.

C: 一台缝纫机，款式精美。
E: An elaborate sewing machine.

C: 「在末日谣言四起之时，大守护者阿丽萨•兰德与她忠诚的筑城者们一道号召众人高筑城墙，成就了如今对抗风雪的最后堡垒——贝洛伯格；」
E: "When the rumors of the doomsday spread, the Supreme Guardian Alisa Rand and her loyal Architects called upon everyone to build a great wall, which has since become the last bastion against the wind and snow — Belobog."

C: 「——『慈母』阿丽娜•兰德。」
E: "— The Loving, Alina Rand."

C: 幽灵星槎也是岁阳造成的吗？
E: Can a heliobus create a phantom starskiff too?

C: 也许是我疑神疑鬼了。这几天她变得和平时有些不一样，总是长吁短叹、多愁善感…就像是变了个人。
E: Perhaps I'm being paranoid, but I do find my mother different from her usual self. She often moans and sighs, seemingly moody all day... It's as if she's become another person.

C: 驭空大人，有件事想和你聊聊…
E: Madam Yukong, there's something we wish to speak with you...

C: 没想到，你也有这样的妈妈。
E: I didn't expect you to have a Mom like this.

C: 我以为自己已经习惯不再怀念了，直到我走进船舱，再次见到你，我又想起了逝去的那些日子，我又变回了软弱的狐人姑娘，只会哭泣。
E: I thought I'd gotten used to not thinking of you. But when I boarded the starskiff and saw you again, I remembered those bygone days and was reduced to a weak foxian girl once more, a little kit who could do nothing but cry.

C: 不行，这些都是独一无二的回忆！
E: No, those were all unique memories!

C: （看来它们真的很喜欢拍照……）
E: (Looks like they really love photography...)

C: 啊？只是路人啊…真是的，白高兴一场。
E: Oh, just passing by... Really, I got excited about nothing.

C: 之前真是吓死我了，还以为这些照片再也找不回来了呢。
E: I was so scared that I was never going to be able to get these photos back.

C: 拍摄的目标应该不是桑博，再拍一张吧……
E: Sampo probably isn't the target for this picture, let's take another one...

C: 以前这里很繁华吗？
E: Was this place more prosperous back then?

C: 哎哟，我都不敢想。
E: Haha, don't be so gullible.

C: 小吃的生意倒还算热闹，但其他生意，还有码头的运营却到了捉襟见肘的地步。
E: The food hawkers are thriving, but dock operations and other businesses are stretched to the breaking point.

C: 试砸了，你就没有饭票了喔！
E: What if I mess up? You'd have to wave bye to your snacks.

C: …是这样的吗？请你不要马上拒绝我。
E: ...Is that how you feel? Well, don't turn down the offer just yet.

C: 哈——？你们不会以为这点交易量就能满足了吧？
E: Ha...? You guys didn't think that this transaction volume would be enough, did you?

C: 金人巷的商铺们可不是来陪你们过家家的，大家都是要挣钱的。
E: The shopkeepers in Aurum Alley aren't here to play house with you — we're all trying to turn a profit.

C: 我、我对不起金人巷……
E: I... hereby apologize to Aurum Alley...

C: 客人是来修什么东西的？敝店能打包票，将您的机巧修复如初。
E: Need something fixed? We guarantee that your ingenium will be good as new.

C: 不能够为商铺们着想的商会，是没有意义的。
E: A guild that doesn't proactively help businesses has no reason to exist.

C: 「金人巷推广大使」？
E: Alley ambassador?

C: 这是在金人巷拍的嘛。
E: Well, all the pictures will be shot in the Alley. So there you go.

C: 再帮我想一句宣传口号。
E: Gotta have another slogan for this one.

C: 金人巷，陌生人合影的好地方。
E: Aurum Alley — Come Meet Some Strangers.

C: 就算有很多金人巷商户们期望如此，但这并不代表他们有远见卓识。
E: In agreeing with your approach, those shops may risk missing the bigger picture... and opportunities for future growth.

C: 借助宣传礼品、虚拟购物券等等手段，前往金人巷消费的游客比以往增加了一倍还多。你看，美食街在大家的心中仍然有重要的地位。
E: By sending out coupons and gifts, we attracted twice as many customers as we normally would. This shows that food is still a big reason why people visit the Alley.

C: 不好！
E: You don't know that!

C: 小手段无法获得真正的认可。
E: Cheaters won't prosper.

C: 追问
E: (Continue questioning)

C: 你看看这个。
E: Take a look at this.

C: 这和现在的话题无关吧。
E: This has nothing to do with the topic at hand.

C: （想太多了，干脆直接离开这里）
E: (I'm overthinking this. Just get out of here, right now)

C: 但是你为了帮助我偷运货物，击退了云骑军，真是太靠谱啦。
E: But you even fought off the Cloud Knights just to help me smuggle some goods. You really are reliable!

C: 祝你发财。
E: I wish you great success.

C: 阿姨听金人巷商会的小秘书提起过你，你们商会要用金人巷码头把货发到其他洞天里去。
E: I heard about you from Miss Secretary of the Merchant Guild. The Guild plans to use the Aurum Alley dock to ship goods to other delves.

C: 你不要高看阿姨嘞，燕翠师傅是有点功夫在身上的，我就算了吧。
E: Don't put me on a pedestal. Yancui is supremely talented. Me? Not so much.

C: 住手，我点的是中杯古藤百草饮。
E: Stop. I ordered a medium cup of herbal tea.

C: 你们要能还上，<color=#dbc291ff>就拿出证据来呀</color>。
E: If you have the necessary funds, <color=#dbc291ff>prove it</color>.

C: 这能说明什么？请不要浪费时间。
E: What am I looking at? Please don't waste our time.

C: 哈哈，在谈判桌上就别玩文字游戏了吧。
E: Haha, I think you understand my point.

C: {NICKNAME}，真是多亏了你，寿考堂立刻给金人巷港口补货，你也辛苦了。
E: {NICKNAME}, thanks to you, Longevity Hall can now send supplies over to the Aurum Alley port. Thank you once again!

C: 你肯定见过会说话的机巧鸟，负责任的机巧鸟都说话。
E: Surely you've seen a talking cycrane before. Responsible cycranes can all talk.

C: 你可以叫我「机巧鸟交通事故大师」。
E: You can call me "Maestro Cycrane of Traffic Accidents."

C: 发货仓满了，你应该赶紧发货。
E: Storage has reached capacity. You should hurry and start shipping.

C: 狠狠摇晃箱子！
E: Shake the box hard!

C: 下班时间到！冲冲冲！
E: Great! Time to get off work!

C: 这人恐怕是来胡搅蛮缠的，大家快跑！
E: This person is a saboteur! Run away!

C: 你说得没错，问题的确就出在咱家的外卖配送一事上……
E: Exactly, something is terribly wrong with the delivery...

C: （捡起外卖食盒）
E: (Pick up the lunch box.)

C: 哎，不必客气，你这块璞玉，世玲稍加指点，以后也能在科研圈发光发热！
E: No need to be modest. With my brilliant guidance, even someone as plain as you can have a bright future as a researcher.

C: 这「感应仪」看着不太结实…
E: The sensor doesn't seem very sturdy...

C: 哎呀！别看了！快走吧！
E: Stop! Don't look for it! Let's go!

C: 专业的就好！毕竟我也是专业的科学家，团队里的其他人也得是专业的才行！
E: Professional is good! I'm also a professional scientist, so my colleagues have to be professional as well!

C: 哎！又回到最开始了！我的问题还没回答呢！
E: Ah! He looped back! And still hasn't answered my question!

C: 以后我肯定会守护好我的研究笔记，再也不会让它们抢走了！
E: I'm gonna protect my research notes extremely well now. They won't ever be stolen again!

C: 那就这么决定了，{NICKNAME}经理！希望在你的协助下，博物馆能实现稳步复兴。
E: Let's do it, Manager {NICKNAME}! I hope the museum will be revived steadily with your help!

C: 发出一声安慰的*哼*。
E: Let out a reassuring grunt.

C: 因为要喝苏乐达。
E: The prisoners' craving for SoulGlad.

C: 很好，很好。我们现在有动力、有脑子、也有——
E: Good, very good. Now we've got motivation, brains, and...

C: 很好，调查到目前为止一切顺利——起码我们俩都还走得动路，也没有缺胳膊少腿。
E: Good, the investigation has been going well so far. At least we can still walk, and we haven't lost any limbs.

C: 今晚的主题是！香蕉皮之夜！
E: Our theme for tonight is "Night of the Banana Peel"!

C: 好！大！一！块！生！肉！就让我来烤烤你！
E: IT'S RAW!!!!!! WHAT! A! DREADFUL! CHUNK! OF! RAW! MEAT! Do I need to put you on a grill!?

C: 哈哈，这不是能者多劳嘛。你不来当这里的调饮师可实在太浪费了。
E: Haha, with great talent comes great responsibility! It would be a waste if you didn't work as a drinksmith here.

C: 你回应得也很不错。能接纳这些怪物，和它们平等交流…我就很感激了。
E: And you respond to their issues well. I'm already grateful because you can accept these monsters and treat them as equals.

C: 所以…就砸了那只傻狗的东西……
E: So that's why... I smashed that idiot pooch's thing...

C: 这么做也不对吧？
E: You shouldn't have done that, right?

C: 现在就回去了！告辞！
E: Well, I'm taking my leave! See you!

C: 给我看看呗。
E: Show me what you've got.

C: 别忘了还有人在等你。
E: Don't forget that someone is waiting for you.

C: 新来的调饮师，快来帮忙哇——小星星失去理智了汪！
E: Come help, new drinksmith! Starlet has lost his mind!

C: 怪物们纷纷离开了酒吧，没过多久……
E: The monsters all left the lounge one after another, and not long after that...

C: 这段时间辛苦了。我还会来光顾的。
E: Thank you for what you've done. I'll drop by next time.

C: 我…
我最近在酒吧里的时间太少了，很多事也没来得及向你们解释。抱歉。
E: I'm sorry. I haven't been spending much time at the lounge lately, and I haven't had a chance to explain everything to you guys.

C: 又来了两个？别妨碍我们执行任务，不管你是谁，都阻止不了猎犬家系！
E: Two more? Don't get in our way! Whoever you are, don't think you can stop the Bloodhound Family!

C: （怎么包裹还在桌子上？）
E: (Why is the package still on the table?)

C: 里面应该藏着不少大鱼，真好奇他们看到这份名单时的表情。
E: A few big fish have gotta be in there. I wonder what their faces would look like when they see this list.

C: 他也不嫌闷得慌。
E: Won't he get bored?

C: 听说闹鬼的电梯恢复了平静，看来您已经找到一份隐藏起来的宝物了……
E: It seems like the haunted lift has regained its peace and quiet. Looks like you've found a hidden treasure already...

C: 这个…宾客，我不是不想相信您，但梦中有太多虚假的事——口说无凭，请拿出一些证据吧。
E: Well... It's not that I find you untrustworthy, but there are simply too many things that are untrue in dreams. I'd appreciate it if you could show me some proof.

C: 多亏了票房的收益，我们修复工程也重新启动了——看，现在的影城已经好多了！
E: We also get to resume the refurbishment project for the theme park with the box office earnings... Look, the place is in much better shape now!

C: 没问题。
E: No problem.

C: 不…这部影片的导演是「钟表匠」，也只能是「钟表匠」。
E: No... The director of this film is the Watchmaker. It can't possibly be anyone else.

C: 「钟表匠」…唉……
E: Watchmaker... Alas...

C: 尝试再次洞察她的内心。
E: Try reading her thoughts again.

C: 你说的「他」是谁？
E: Who's "he"?

C: 在这部《美梦大机密》中，钟表小子与过去的伙伴发生了冲突，情节也走向了一个并不美好的方向。
E: In Dreamjoy Secrecy, Clockie has a fallout with his companions and the plot takes on a dark turn.

C: 用这样的方式记录过去，还真是有趣，让我想起了一位同样喜欢将记忆留在镜头中的忆者……
E: To use such a method to document the past is interesting. It reminds me of a Memokeeper who also loves to record their memories on the camera...

C: 你别说，我以前一直以为这圆盘脑袋是给没断奶的小孩儿看的，没想到做的还真不错。
E: Well I'll be hornswoggled, I reckoned them clock-heads were for young 'uns fresh off the teat. Turns out, they sure did a fine job with 'em.

C: 那就这样吧，亲爱的导演——我谨代表克劳克影业向您致敬，我们绝不会忘记您为「钟表匠」先生还有《钟表小子》付出的一切努力！
E: Now then, dear director — On behalf of Clock Studios, I salute you! We'll never forget all the hard work you put in for Mr. Watchmaker and the Clockie series!

C: <color=#dbc291ff>《哈努兄弟大劫案》</color>。故事仍以美梦小镇为背景，主要以我方品牌形象与《美梦往事》中的「哈努兄弟」同框出现，互惠共赢。
E: Brother Hanu's Great Heist. The story is set in Dreamville, and the main thing is to have our brand's imagery and Brother Hanu from Dreamjoy Memoir be in the same shot. It's a win-win for both sides.

C: 「美梦中本不存在痛苦与哭泣，直到石头老板来到小镇。黑心的石头老板为了赚取宝石，没日没夜地压榨工厂中的员工。工厂中到处怨声载道……」
E: "Pain and tears did not exist in dreams until Boss Stone came to the town. The ruthless Boss Stone drove factory workers relentlessly, demanding gems day and night. Within the factory walls, grievances echoed..."

C: 看不见我，看不见我…
E: Can't see me. Can't see me...

C: 「一路走来的同伴们也曾渴求着这份魔法。垃垃斯基希望让自己的垃圾袋内充满智慧、圆幕先生希望自己能有一颗稳定而开朗的内心，而气泡锂犬则希望获得比气泡更加真实的勇气。」
E: "The companions who have traveled far also once thirsted for this magic. Lalasky hopes to fill its own trash bag with wisdom. Mr. Domescreen hopes to have a stable and carefree heart, and the Bubble Hound hopes to gain courage more reliable than a bubble."

C: 「全剧终。」
E: "The end."

C: 记住，要<color=#dbc291ff>让所有演员入镜</color>，明白了吗？
E: Remember, <color=#dbc291ff>all the actors must be in frame</color>. Got that?

C: 嗯？{M#小伙子}{F#小姑娘}<color=#dbc291ff>拍得不错</color>啊。这个镜头过了。这场还有一个镜头。
E: Hmm? <color=#dbc291ff>Not too shabby at all, {M#boy}{F#girl}</color>. This scene is done. There's another scene coming up.

C: 滴答，折纸小鸟，我来救你了！
E: Tick-tock, origami bird, I'm here to save you!

C: 啾，你们要来一场折纸战争吧，最后活下来的人可以实现愿望啾！
E: Tweet, you lot have to undertake an origami battle. The last one standing will be granted a wish, tweet!

C: 不，一定有我们能够一起出去的方法！难道说…只能用掉那个了……
E: No, there must be some way out! Don't tell me... I have to use that...

C: 太感谢了！期待您的好消息！
E: Thanks a lot! Looking forward to hearing the good news!

C: 这真是个大胆的设想，不过还挺有教育意义的，屠龙者终成恶龙……
E: It's a bold idea, but one with a nice moral lesson inside it: The dragonslayer becomes the dragon...

C: 哦？那也没事，反正出了问题不用我担责……
E: Huh? That's fine, I don't have to shoulder any responsibility anyway if anything goes wrong...

C: 别装了。
E: Drop the act already.

C: 你向动画师讲述了有关折纸筑梦师的故事……
E: You fill the animation artist in about the Origami Dreamweaver story...

C: 木头老爷外传的广告上映了。
E: The trailer for Old Man Wood's extra story has been released.

C: 真是墙倒众人推…
E: People always try to kick you when you're down...

C: 有点可疑…
E: Sounds kinda sus...

C: 那太好了！目前《银河球棒侠传说》还在研发中，我们需要测试玩家更多的反馈与建议。
E: Fantastic! Right now, Legend of the Galactic Baseballer is still under development, and we need more testing to get more user feedback and suggestions.

C: 游戏机就在这里。别担心，它会自动保存你的进度，随时回来玩都可以。有问题也可以来问我。
E: The game console is right over here. Don't worry, your progress gets auto-saved and you can come back to play any time. Feel free to ask me if you have any questions.

C: 游戏机我留在这，测试结束前都可以随意游玩。
E: I'll leave the gaming console here, and you can keep playing it any time until testing finishes.

C: 后面的游戏内容还在开发中，预计下一次测试时上线。
E: The upcoming content for the game is still in development and is expected to go online by the next test.

C: 听说你有事找我？
E: I heard you are looking for me?

C: （离开）
E: (Leave)

C: 但此时，你的搭档却仰望天空，闭上双眼。雨滴落下，视野也一片模糊，她悲伤地说——
E: But at this moment, your partner yet gazes into the sky, both her eyes closed. The rain drops fall, blurring her vision, and she tragically says...

C: 你闭上双眼，耳边响起送葬的哀歌。你感到自己逐渐下沉，远离真实世界，但过去擦肩而过的死亡，却逐渐迫近。
E: You close your eyes and hear an elegy. You can feel yourself sinking, getting further away from the real world. However, you find the deaths you once narrowly escaped drawing closer.

C: 感受如何，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}？
E: How did it feel, {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}?

C: …不知道你在说什么。
E: ...I have no idea what you're talking about.

C: 你真是三月七吗？
E: Are you really March 7th?

C: 他们会看到，当初被当众羞辱的斯科特重新回到了金人巷一雪前耻。
E: They'll see that I, Skott, who was once shamed in front of everyone, is back in Aurum Alley to restore his honor!
Whew...

C: 怎么不算呢？野生动物不是比这家伙善良多了？
E: What would you call it, then? Even a feral wolf is better than what he was, right?

C: 快跑啊！斯科特专员发狂咬人了！
E: Run for your life! Specialist Skott has gone insane!

C: 跟岁阳相关的事务没之前那么多了，可是别的事情又开始忙起来了……
E: There are fewer heliobus-related jobs than before, though they've been replaced with other matters...

C: 两位再见！
E: Goodbye!

C: 三月七只觉眼前一黑，数不尽的拳脚功夫在一瞬间涌入脑海。
E: All March 7th can see before her is darkness, countless martial arts of myriad unarmed styles all surging into her mind.

C: 再见藿藿！
E: Goodbye Huohuo!

C: 那不会。丹鼎司做手术都用它，能有什么副作用？只不过……
E: No, not at all. We use it for surgeries in the Alchemy Commission, so what kind of side effects could it possibly have? It's just...
Wait, what???

C: 紧张什么？对付几个公司的杂鱼，很简单的。
E: Nervous about what? Dealing with a few IPC minions is just a walk in the park.

C: 嘻嘻，「知己知彼，对手狗殆。」
E: Hehe. "Know yourself and know your enemy, and you'll never have to sleep in the doghouse."

C: 交给我吧！我是谈判专家！
E: Leave it to me! I'm an expert negotiator!

C: 十七轮？！那也有点太恐怖了！要不是我已经进了公司……
E: Seventeen rounds? That's insane! If I hadn't joined the IPC already...

C: 我需要发自内心地向她道歉。
E: I see. I'll make a genuine, heartfelt apology to her.

C: …这盒子都装着什么药呀？
E: ...What kind of medicine is stored in this box?

C: 什么是黑玉龟苓膏？
E: What's Obsidian Herbal Jelly?

C: 你和三月七忐忑地饱餐了一顿，体会到「染指派」的真髓。
E: You and March 7th have an uneasy meal, experiencing the real meaning behind the Ranzhi School.

C: 呵呵，增长功力的灵丹妙药？椒丘这家伙……
E: Heh-heh, a miracle elixir that can boost your skills? That rascal Jiaoqiu...

C: 老夫可是以太战线的老玩家了。
E: Yes, I've been playing Aetherium Wars for a long time.

C: 如果是重要的消息，我们是不是回避一下比较好？
E: If it's something important, should we excuse ourselves?

C: 什么乱七八糟的机械祷词？
E: What nonsensical mechanical prayer is this?

C: 你们怎么盯着我看，怎么样了，降灵术成功了吗？
E: Why are all of you staring at me? What happened? Did the seance succeed?

C: 没有太卜大人的太卜司，看上去有些寂寞啊。
E: The Divination Commission seems so lonely without the Master Diviner around.

C: 别问，旷世神功都是有代价的。
E: Naturally all powerful techniques come with a cost.

C: 书库里收着的残篇多半是云骑军研发武道的失败品，不但有可能要配上类似药王秘传这般的禁忌药物改造身体，还得配合各种危险的武器……
E: The majority of the incomplete texts in the repository are failed examples of martial research and development. Not only do they require combinations with forbidden potions like those found in the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, some of them even require all sorts of dangerous weapons.

C: 这小姑娘定是不小心翻开了禁书。青雀你先把书阖上，妾身会准备好焚香将她唤醒。
E: This girl must have accidentally opened one of the forbidden texts. Qingque, you close the book up, and I will prepare an incense to bring her to her senses.

C: 看来三月姑娘终于醒过来了。
E: I see you're finally awake, Miss March.

C: 放下助人情结，尊重他人命运。
E: You have to release this need to help others and leave them to their fate.

C: 我这人有些小市民情结，尽管有时会憧憬大英雄，但我也不禁会想，是不是没有命悬一线、险象环生的经历更加适合自己。
E: I can be a bit of a philistine sometimes. Though I sometimes long for the life of a hero, I always think to myself, maybe I'm more suited to a life that isn't fraught with danger where I'm constantly surviving by the skin of my teeth?

C: 在准备充分之后，三月七从故事结尾开始往前阅读禁书。
E: After making time to prepare herself, March 7th began reading the forbidden text from the end of the story.

C: 这些人类无意义的精神碎片——忆质，在街头巷尾。我开发了一个成像程序，让这些离散的忆质团块能呈现在你的相机镜头里。
E: These are the undervalued psychological fragments of people — memoria, roaming the streets and alleys. I have developed an imaging program that makes these scattered memoria clumps visible to your camera lens.

C: 开始今日拍摄。
E: Let's begin today's shoot.

C: 你有办法离开夹层世界吗？
E: Is there a way for you to leave the Betweenwhere?

C: 卢卡选手，你的身上果然充满了新闻价值。我决定持续跟踪报道你的赛事进程。
E: Luka, you are absolutely radiating with newsworthiness. I've decided to follow you and report on your progress throughout the competition.

C: 为什么大家长得都看不出年龄啊？我找一个尖耳朵小姑娘问路，叫了一声「小妹妹」，结果人家生气了，说自己已经三百四十多岁了…搞得我现在都不敢和人说话了。
E: Why doesn't anyone look their age? I approached this girl with pointy ears to ask for directions and I called her "little girl" and she got mad! She said she's over three hundred and forty years old... So now I'm even afraid to talk to people.

C: 玲可去考古了，她想通过那块宝石挂坠了解伊戈尔回雅利洛-Ⅵ前的历史。史瓦罗大佬带着虎克去别处玩了——他真的蛮会带孩子的。
E: Lynx went on an archaeological escapade: She wants to try to learn about Igor's history before he returned to Jarilo-VI with that gemmed pendant. Svarog took Hook out to play — he's really good with kids.

C: 如果是压力太大的话…不如喝一瓶好梦气泡饮吧！这是苏乐达的仙舟版，还有安神助眠的功效～「喝下美梦气泡水，嘴里甜来心里美！」
E: If you're too stressed out... How about a bottle of Sweet Dreams Soda! This is the Xianzhou version of SoulGlad, and it helps you sleep better too! "Grab some Sweet Dreams Soda, a sweetness that's bound to last!"

C: 不过就算差距再大，我也必须挥拳、必须战斗、必须胜利！
E: But no matter how wide that gap is, I must... wield my fists... I must fight, and I must win!

C: 好久不见，{NICKNAME}。我也没想到会在这里见到你。
E: Long time no see, {NICKNAME}. I wasn't expecting to see you here.

C: 不知道该说什么。
E: I don't know what to say.

C: 它蕴含着一个机会，让整个银河再一次认识我们——一个从军团与寒潮中坚强幸存的英雄国度。
E: It symbolizes a chance to introduce ourselves to the cosmos once again — a heroic kingdom that survived both the Legion and the Eternal Freeze with tenacity.

C: 而红方选手竟然是，星际和平公司记录在案的通缉重犯——「巡海游侠」波提欧！
E: Fighting out of the red corner, it's none other than the notorious felon wanted by the IPC, the Galaxy Ranger — Boothill!

C: 噢～我说参赛者名单上怎么没你呢。我还以为你他宝贝的在这儿埋伏我呢！
E: Oh! No wonder I didn't see your name on the list. I thought you were gonna fudgin' ambush me here!

C: 号外，号外！比咱们藿藿还弱的怂包终于给找着了！
E: Extra! Extra! Brat even more cowardly than Huohuo discovered! Read all about it!

C: 既然这小子放弃比赛了，那{NICKNAME}，你就把藿藿好好操练操练，别惦记这些破事了。
E: If that kid wants to forfeit the match, {NICKNAME}, why don't you give Huohuo some proper training and forget about this mess?

C: 你这丫头！这保密意识还当判官呢！算了，回去再和你算账。
E: You rascal! How are you even a Judge when you can't even keep a secret! Agh forget it! I'll deal with you when we get back.

C: 真希望有一天，我也能变得和卢卡哥哥一样强大！
E: I really hope I can grow up to be strong like Luka one day!

C: 伊戈尔的事情暂时告一段落了。我们回休息室准备接下来的叩关赛吧。
E: I guess that's all there is about Igor for now. Let's head back to the lounge to prepare for the upcoming knockout stage.

C: 我？可我已经输了啊？将军也对落败的选手感兴趣吗？
E: Me? But I lost the match. Is the general really interested in meeting defeated contestants too?

C: 我明白了，谢谢你，景元。
E: I understand. Thank you, Jing Yuan.

C: …我要回来了，妈妈。
E: ...I'm coming back, Mother.

C: 没错。她涉嫌犯下多起谋杀罪行，同时伪造身份、携带高危武器入境。
E: That's right. She's suspected of committing multiple murders, of identity fraud, and of smuggling highly dangerous weapons into Xianzhou territory.

C: 会不会是个误会？
E: Could this be a misunderstanding?

C: 我都能轻易将他抹杀。
E: I can easily wipe away his existence.

C: 小瞧狐人会吃大亏的。
E: You'll lose out if you underestimate foxians.

C: 似乎是他压力过大的原因…
E: It appears to be because he's under a lot of stress...

C: 例行的采访环节已经结束，接下来该去办正事了。
E: Now that the routine interview is over, it's time to get down to business.

C: 我们仨就是大名鼎鼎的星核猎手！平时最大的爱好就是战斗、毁灭、吃小孩！
E: The three of us are the infamous Stellaron Hunters! Our favorite hobbies include fighting, destruction, and devouring children.

C: 哼哼，别担心，他马上就乐不出来了！
E: Heh-heh, don't worry. He won't be cackling much after this!

C: 其实我关注到近期网络上的一些言论说两位选手都来自贝洛伯格，并且关系不错，有没有可能是史瓦罗选手故意放水…{NICKNAME}教练对此有何解释？
E: Reading recent comments on the net, they're saying that both contestants are from Belobog, and enjoy a cordial relationship. Is there a possibility that Svarog may have pulled his punches...? What does Coach {NICKNAME} have to say?

C: 我的战术用完了…
E: I've already used up all my tactics...

C: 卢卡选手扭转了战局！太强了！
E: Luka has turned the tables! Legendary!

C: 卢卡退赛
E: Luka needs to quit.

C: 这不算什么。
E: It's nothing.

C: 别担心，我还没死。我只是将自己的形象授权给了「罗浮庙会筹备组」，所以他们可以让虚拟大毫担任庙会引导员。
E: Don't worry. I'm not dead. I've just transferred my image rights to the Luofu Temple Fair Preparatory Group so they can use my virtual emulation as a temple fair guide.

C: 所以，你能带我们找到这幅涂鸦的作者吗？
E: So, can you take us to this graffiti artist?

C: 虽然很期待你的舞台表现，可惜，这次似乎没有机会呢。
E: While I look forward to your stage performance, it appears that I may not have that chance this time.

C: 不管你有没有听过，反正不是我瞎说。乐队经理由我当，大家谁都不用慌。三场演出才算完，各位都还有得忙。难！
E: Doesn't matter if you've heard this before, I'm not kidding around anyway. I'm the band manager, so no one needs to panic. We've got three shows to do, so everyone's got to get busy. It's gonna be hard!

C: 当然。要是随便用现成的乐曲就能完成演出，这场派对的水准也未免太低了。
E: Of course. If any existing piece could be used to complete the show, the quality of this party would be rock-bottom.

C: 音律•忍法看似种类繁多，然奥义极为简单——「旋律乃是记录世界，绝非凭空编造。」
E: There may be different Sound Release ninjutsu styles, but our essence is very simple: "Rhythm is a record of the world, not conjured from thin air."

C: 你看着蒙塔娜的手指以极快的速度在屏幕上敲击起来，终于明白了为何原始博士没有选择将人类变为其他动物——有手实在太重要了。
E: You watch as Montana's fingers rapidly tap on the screen, finally realizing why Dr. Primitive didn't choose to turn humans into other animals — fingers are really handy.

C: <size=36>喔！！！</size>
E: <size=36>YEAAAAH!!!</size>

C: 演出的水准确实很高，即使像我这样专业的乐评人也挑不出什么毛病。
E: The show's caliber was outstanding. Even a seasoned music critic like me had nothing to nitpick.

C: 喔！「缭乱」！「缭乱」！我喜欢你们的演出！盛会巨星也来和我一起欢呼吧！
E: Wahoo! Dazzling! Dazzling! I love your performance! Come cheer with me, Festive Superstar!

C: 乐队正在演奏美梦小镇的庆典进行曲——好梦来啾，要努力跟上我们的节奏啾！
E: The band's playing the Dreamville's marching song — Good Dreams Come, tweet! Do your best to keep up with us, tweet!

C: ——让我和盛会巨星的乐队一起，尽情享受这场音乐派对吧！
E: ...Let me fully enjoy this music party with the festive superstar's band!

C: 啊，不会是…大家都被邪恶的怪物偷偷替换掉了，整个空间站只剩我一个活人了吧？！
E: Ah, could it be... everyone was replaced by some evil monster, and I'm the only one alive in this whole space station!?

C: 加入垃圾桶的示威。
E: Join the trashcans' demonstration.

C: 偷偷跟两位说，我和他们只是逢场作戏，不算数的。真要说起来，我的心思都在乐队上。
E: I'll let you both in on a secret. I'm really just playing along with these guys. They don't mean anything to me. If I'm honest, my heart is still with the band.

C: 若真是如此，阁下为何要丢下诸音忍，执意离开？
E: If that were the case, why were you so intent on abandoning your fellow sound ninjas?

C: 哎，说实话吧，玩乐队这事，靠的就是热情。要是没兴趣，谁会愿意花几十个系统时躲在房间里练习，只为了台上那短短几分钟的演奏？
E: Ah, I'll be honest with you. Being in a band is all about passion, right? If you're not invested, why would you sit alone in your room wasting dozens of system hours practicing just for a few minutes on stage?

C: 音乐派对结束后，能否申请让校方接纳达凡绮作为正式的乐队成员？
E: Once the music party is concluded, will you be able to apply to the academy for Dafanchy to become an official member of the band?

C: 主公。我等出战的时机，仍旧交由你把握了。
E: Master, the decision of when to send us out to the contest remains in your hands.

C: （乐队成员都沉浸在音乐的世界里，还是不要再打扰了。）
E: (The band members are immersed in the world of music. Best not to interrupt.)

C: 要是谐乐学院教出来的是这种玩意，我还不如去街边找几个自动乐团来当学生！
E: If Charmony Academy was responsible for teaching such garbage, I might as well admit an automated orchestra off the streets!

C: 要不还是你们演吧。
E: Why not you guys perform instead?

C: 等等——不是！小矮个，我可从来没让你开除艾尔菲啊！
E: Hang on, wait! Shortie, I never told you to expel Arfey!

C: 咕咕，让我给你讲个故事吧——关于这片折纸树林的故事。
E: Hoot hoot, let me tell you a story — about Paperfold Woods.

C: 咕。可恶，我们和你不是宿敌吗！
E: Ugh. Blast, aren't we archrivals!?

C: 很明显，这只扑满相当喜欢你饱含爱意的抚摸，并热情地向你示好。
E: It's rather obvious that this trotter loves your affectionate caress. It enthusiastically reciprocates your warmth.

C: 大哥！我终于找到了啊，属于我们要走的道路。加把劲，挚友！
E: Big Bro! I've finally found it! A path for us to walk on! Give it your all, my bosom friend!

C: 吉他•忍徒
E: Ninja Initiate Guitar

C: 为什么放弃音乐了呢？
E: Why did you give up on music?

C: 为什么放弃音乐了呢？
E: Why did you give up on music?

C: 调侃归调侃，老桑博我最欣赏纯美骑士了。世上只有两种人像他们一样坚定，一种是傻子，一种——是愚者。
E: I jest, but the Knights of Beauty are most deserving of my utmost admiration. There are only two types of people as steadfast as them: Idiots and fools.

C: 必须阻止她！
E: We must stop her!

C: 咖啡渣。
E: Coffee grounds.

C: 很荣幸，但…我们还有这种奖项吗？
E: I'm honored, but... was there such an award before?

C: 播放《灾虐的黎明》。
E: Play Dawn of Disaster.

C: 谨慎为妙。
E: Best be on your guard.

C: 半价礼金！
E: Half off, guys!

C: 呜呜…我睡姿比较差嘛……
E: Mmmh... I just have bad sleeping posture...

C: 你可真是个武林高手。
E: You're a real martial expert.

C: 有种科研般的严谨呢。
E: That feels awfully specific, real academic-like.

C: 你和三月七面面相觑。出于好奇，你们等到了重磅嘉宾「星穷列车」入场……
E: You and March 7th give each other a dumbfounded stare. Out of curiosity, you wait for the entrance of these heavy-duty guests, the Arstral Express...

C: 前扑！
E: Lunge!

C: 怎么咋咋呼呼的。别害怕，是我，小粉毛。
E: Stop causing a scene. Don't worry. It's just me, Pink Hair.

C: 上半场演出马上就要结束了，咱们赶紧去抓拍几张！
E: The first half is about to end. We have to take a few photos!

C: 哼哼，拍幽囚狱！大家感兴趣又接触不到的东西，才是最吸引人的，就像这儿。
E: Heh-heh, the Shackling Prison, of course! Everyone's most captivated by things they are curious about but can't reach — just like this place.

C: 这就是无名客的身体素质！
E: That's the physique of the Nameless!

C: 桀桀桀！等你们有本事抓住我再说吧，溜了——
E: Tsk tsk! Only if you two can catch me! I'm off!

C: <size=28>别喊了，快停下！！！</size>
E: <size=28>Stop yelling! Pipe down now!!!</size>

C: 死亡有何可怖？赴战场，垂英名，纵使葬于他乡，也胜过苟延残喘的安详。
E: What terror is there in death? Charging into battle to stake your heroic claim, falling in a land far from home — that is an ending that triumphs over a mundane existence in peace.

C: 「…晨昏之眼看见一切，其目光如天穹般深远。」
E: "...The Eye of Twilight is all-seeing, its vision as far-reaching as the sky dome..."

C: 我族是艾格勒在人间的眼睛，即便它已阖上百眼，我们依然坚守。
E: My clan is the eye of Aquila in the mortal realm. Even though they have closed their hundred eyes, we still persist in our duties.

C: 你好…这位先生，小奇美拉已经道过歉，就请别训斥它们了。这种严苛的管教方式，是不会得到好结果的呀。
E: Hello sir... these little chimeras already apologized, so please stop berating them. Such harsh disciplinary treatment will never result in positive outcomes.

C: 这位先生，「天空」祭司间流传着一句谚语，叫做——等{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}睁开眼睛。意思就是：在天亮前，别轻易做出判断。
E: Sir, there is a saying passed down among the Sky Priests, which is... Wait for {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila {RUBY_E#} to open their eyes. It means: Don't make hasty judgments before the sun rises.

C: 啊，灰宝，我想了想，要不管理员的位置还是交给你吧。
E: Ah, Grayie, I gave it some thought — how about you be their administrator?

C: 因为，{F#她}{M#他}比你有价值。
E: Because, {F#she}{M#he} is more valuable than you.

C: 嗷呜！提前下班~开心~可以玩~嗷呜嗷呜！
E: Awoo! Getting off work early, so happy! We can go play, awoo-awoo!

C: 也帮帮我吧。
E: Help me too.

C: 从诞生之初，它们就被赋予了为人们服务的使命。想想…还挺难过的。
E: From the moment they are born, they are bequeathed the mission to serve humans. Just thinking about it... it makes me sad.

C: 学医救不了奥赫玛。
E: Medicine won't save Okhema.

C: 不用紧张。小灰，小风堇，我来帮你们。
E: Don't panic. Little Gray, Cinny, I will help you.

C: 时间到了，可以点投降了吧？
E: Time's up. Can I surrender now?

C: 管理之道，就在其中——
E: Success to management, is within...

C: 我同意大家的看法，奇美拉是种相当聪明的生物，很认真，也很有热情，愿意为奥赫玛的居民们解决麻烦。能取得如此成绩，都是它们的功劳。
E: I agree with everyone that chimeras are smart creatures — dedicated, passionate, and willing to solve troubles for Okhema's people. It is all thanks to their efforts that we've achieved such success.

C: 嗯。奇美拉忙碌了这么久，当然该好好休息下，那些委托在你有兴趣的时候来处理就好。
E: Of course. After all the hard work, the chimeras have earned some rest. The assignments can wait until you're ready.

C: 无论白天，还是夜晚~♪ ♪
E: Be it day or night~ ♪ ♪

C: 接下来的路就只剩王之{RUBY_B#回家}「归乡」{RUBY_E#}了。
E: Time for the royal {RUBY_B#to go home}homecoming{RUBY_E#} now.

C: 好好好…毕竟巴特鲁斯也耽误了不少时间，我宣布比赛立刻开始！
E: Alright, alright, fine... Bartholos did waste a lot of time, so let the match... begin!

C: 你好认真，不像我只想靠队友。
E: You're putting in some serious effort, while I'm just riding on my teammate's coattails.

C: 咱这比赛上的热闹已经够多了！
E: Well, we've got plenty of that to go around here!

C: 我家的泡泡，成长了不少呀…
E: My Bubbles grew up so much...

C: 经过这一场战斗，泡泡已经完全理解了「胜利」的真正含义。
E: After this battle, I fully comprehend the real meaning of "victory."

C: 有输有赢才是竞技比赛嘛。
E: Losing and winning is part of competition.

C: 嗯嗯！面对强大的「诡计」之半神，我们要以「信任」击败她！
E: Mmm! Facing the mighty demigod of "Trickery," we'll defeat her with the power of "trust"!

C: 无刺有刺，搁这玩乐队呢？
E: Non-spiky, spiky — what is this, a band?

C: 小白居然还没念对，是「豹豹碰碰大作战」啦！
E: Snowy still didn't get it right, it's Seal Slammers!

C: 感兴趣！我想要奖励！
E: I'm interested! I want rewards!

C: 这，是你的<color=#dbc291ff>预言梦</color>。而你{NICKNAME}，是「预言之子」。
E: This is your <color=#dbc291ff>prophetic dream</color>. And you, {NICKNAME}, are the "Child of Prophecy."

C: <color=#eb4d3dff>不参加比赛，就无法得到奖杯。没有奖杯，你将永远变成小海豹。</color>
E: <color=#eb4d3dff>No participation means no trophy. No trophy means you will turn into a baby seal and stay that way forever. Forever.</color>

C: 好搭档，喂——别走神了。
E: Hey partner. Are you still here?

C: 太好了，我就知道你会来参加！
E: Wonderful! I knew you'd come through.

C: 你看看这「此世全部之恶」…
E: Take a look at "All the World's Evil" here...

C: 在翁法罗斯的历史里，也有过太多无论预言真假，都可能造成「真实的冲突」的事情。
E: In Amphoreus's history, there were plenty of things that could cause "real conflicts," regardless if the prophecies were true or not.

C: 虽然泡泡戴着墨镜你可能看不见，但它已经泪眼朦胧了。
E: Although I'm wearing sunglasses, and you might not notice, tears are welling in my eyes.

C: 我还有大项目要谈，先走了。
E: I should get going — got some important business to discuss.

C: 既然这是寄给{NICKNAME}的礼物，不如就由{F#她}{M#他}来主玩吧。在这方面，我相信{NICKNAME}的判断。
E: Since it's a gift for {NICKNAME}, why don't we let {F#her}{M#him} be the main player? In this aspect, I trust {NICKNAME}'s judgment.

C: 请别急着下论断，我亲爱的朋友，关于这点…无论是艾普瑟隆的版权保护条例，还是匹诺康尼的知识产权登记表中，我们都没有找到关于「银河球棒侠」的登记。
E: Please don't jump to conclusions, my dear friend. About that... we haven't been able to find any registration for Galactic Baseballer in either Epsilon's copyright protection ordinances or in the Penacony registry of IP.

C: 怎么嘛，我中奖的旅游地点就是这里，我又不是自己选的。
E: Hey, I won a free trip here! It's not like I picked this place myself.

C: 凡是来「晖长石号 / 开拓之尾号 / 塔塔洛夫号 / 飞翔时针号」的游客，都将有机会免费体验它。真是太棒了，不是吗？
E: Anyone who visits The Radiant Feldspar / The Trailblaze's Stern / The Tatalov / The Soaring Clock Hand gets to experience it for free! Amazing, right?

C: 抱歉，青雀女士。我与波提欧兄弟并非破除邪祟的「专家」，帮不了你多少。
E: My apologies, Miss Qingque. Neither Boothill nor I specialize in exorcism, so there's only so much we can do.

C: 其实…遇上这等厄运的是我，啊…还有波提欧先生。
E: Actually... I'm the curse magnet here, and... poor Mr. Boothill too.

C: 原来如此！看来{NICKNAME}•忍者已经识破了恶灵式神•妖鸟的弱点。
E: Brilliant! Ninja {NICKNAME} has already figured out the Evil Spirit Demon Bird's weakness!

C: 啾…啾啾……
E: Tweet... tweet tweet...

C: 真是意外的提问，当然可以。
E: Well, that's unexpected. I see no reason to refuse.

C: 把闲逛说得如此风度翩翩，黑塔女士真是智慧超群！
E: Only Madam Herta could make aimless wandering sound so refined. Truly a mind beyond compare.

C: 你朝走廊的尽头张望，想一睹为快。回廊空空荡荡的，没有透露任何秘密。
E: You peek down the hallway, curious to catch a glimpse. But it's empty and reveals no secret.

C: 人生起起落落，不能期待每一刻都像节日那般精彩。
E: Life rises and falls. You can't expect every moment to feel like a celebration.

C: 提问：星核猎手的藏身处在公司的打击下化为一片焦炭，为什么只有银狼的电脑安然无恙？
E: Question: The Stellaron Hunters' hideout was burned to ashes by an IPC strike, so why was Silver Wolf's computer the only thing left intact?

C: 配方试得差不多了。
E: Most of the recipes have been tested out.

C: 唉…{NICKNAME}，劳烦你替我调上一杯，润润喉咙吧。
E: *sigh* ...{NICKNAME}, please mix me a drink to moisten my throat.

C: 美梦？待机状态人偶的后台会如何运行，这个问题我的确还没仔细考虑过。
E: Dreams? Never studied how Puppet OS runs during standby...

C: 这…危不危险你不都感受到了吗？
E: Can't... Can't you tell already?

C: 哎！您有所不知，观众时间宝贵，耐心是越发少了，大家恨不得一分钟看完一部戏。故事推进太慢可是创作大忌。
E: Ah, you don't understand. You see, the audience's time is precious, and their patience ever thinning. People want it all to be over in a minute, a whole show. Slow-paced storytelling is a big no-no.

C: 这药名一听就是假药吧？！
E: That name sounds totally fake!

C: 在这种地方设计无意义的惊险情节，是三流编剧所为，在下可不屑于如此。
E: Dropping in a meaningless crisis element here would be the work of a hack and a fraud. I think not.

C: 咱说书先生，不就靠这个来赚钱糊口吗？
E: Isn't that how we storytellers earn our crust, day in day out?

C: 这是要是能靠「通融一下」就解决的问题，持明族也不至于落到如今这般田地了。
E: If this was something that could just be "bent," then the Vidyadhara wouldn't find ourselves in our current predicament.

C: 丹恒
E: Dan Heng

C: 「可正当两人沉浸于新生的喜悦中时，危险却悄然靠近。一群药王秘传找到了这里。」
E: "But just as the two were immersed in the joys of life reborn, crisis loomed imminent. A horde of Disciples of Sanctus Medicus had found this location."

C: 好的丹恒 / 冷面小青龙 / 龙尊MK<unbreak>2000</unbreak>型 / 赵大宝老师，您所言极是。{NICKNAME}，人家米糕没相中了咱们写的这个评书，想买下幻戏改编版权，所以让丹恒 / 冷面小青龙 / 龙尊MK<unbreak>2000</unbreak>型 / 赵大宝老师莅临指导。
E: Alright then, Dan Heng / Cold Dragon Young / High Elder MK<unbreak>2000</unbreak> / John Doe. You are emphatically correct. {NICKNAME}, the Mellow-Golden-Mayor Immersia Club picked up our story and is interested in potentially buying the adaptation rights. So let's show Dan Heng / Cold Dragon Young / High Elder MK<unbreak>2000</unbreak> / John Doe a very warm welcome, okay?

C: 西衍先生拿起他的稿本，奋笔疾书。
E: Mr. Xiyan took out his notebook and began scribbling at a furious pace.

C: 算了，既然你只关心那些无聊的「特殊委派」，那我就给你解释解释吧。
E: Forget it. Since you only care about those boring "assignments," let me explain.

C: 那就不出摊，省力又省心。
E: You could shut down the dine-in stall and save the trouble.

C: 不会的，仙舟上念旧的人多。
E: No worries. I'm sure your regulars will still come to your stall.

C: ——有是有，也就那么几段编排好的，比我兜里的钱还干净。
E: We've put on a few decent shows, but that's all about it. To be frank, we've had fewer successful performances than the coins in my pocket.

C: 亡羊补牢也不算晚，况且，能给西衍先生热场子，这可是想都不敢想的机会啊。
E: It's never too late to make things right. Plus, I'd do anything for a chance to give a warm-up performance for Mr. Xiyan.

C: 我这小本买卖，研发成本有限，「几种」真是说得轻松……
E: I'm running a small business and my budget is limited. I can't just "develop several flavors"...

C: 你睁开眼睛，强烈刺激带来的生理泪水令你眼眶通红，你忽然发现眼前的一切失去了原有的光鲜。巨鲸在你脑海中歌唱…下浮…跳跃…旋转，直至眼前突然漆黑一片。
E: You open your eyes, red from the tears caused by the intense sensations. All the colors in the world fade before your eyes. You hear the song of a giant whale in your head. You feel yourself floating... jumping... and spinning... until everything suddenly goes black in your vision.

C: 所以你就找到了我？
E: That's why you've come to me?

C: 好了，现在你该知道哪个是真古董，哪个是假货了吧？
E: Well, now you should know which is the genuine antique and which is fake, right?

C: 能有这样的实力，一定是伤害了不少弱小的以太灵吧，希望你能好好思考我的话…不多说了，还有许多玩家需要我用战斗去启迪呢。
E: To have power like yours, you must have harmed many weak Aether Spirits. I hope that you will reflect on what I have said... I shall say no more, there are many more players that need me to enlighten them in battle.

C: 每天就这么点时间能玩游戏，居然还输了，呜呜呜……
E: I only get this little time to play every day, and I lost!? Waaaa...

C: 「以太复印」是什么？
E: What's "Aether copying?"

C: 公司竟然也懂「以太复印」技术嘛？我还以为这是朋克洛德的骇客们才玩得转的东西呢。
